### What Session 144 Did
**Deliverable:** LabKey research-doc **Rec #3** (owner pick from the doc's three quick-wins) — **explicit optional API-key authentication for the `Rlabkey` data path, with `.netrc` fallback and a clear fail-fast error**. **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-19
**Status:** **DONE. Strict-TDD (RED → GREEN → REFACTOR; all three phase gates via `AskUserQuestion`).** **0 stakeholder corrections.** Did **NOT** bundle the three quick-wins (FM #18/#25): posed a pre-RED **scope** `AskUserQuestion` (owner picked Rec #3 of {version floor, config defaults, API-key auth}), then a pre-RED **approach** `AskUserQuestion` for the author's-call design (credential source = env var `NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY` precedence then config `apiKey` token; `getDemographics()` auto-configures = fail-fast), then the three TDD phase gates. Right-sized **SOLO** under ultracode — a single small, cohesive TDD feature against one package; fanning out file-mutating agents would risk conflicts and add no confidence over firsthand verification. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work).
  - **REAL WORK #1 — grounded the design firsthand BEFORE RED (found two load-bearing constraints):** read `getSiteInfo.R`, `getDemographics.R`, `getConfigFileName.R`, `getTokenList.R`, `getParamDef.R`, the example config, `test_getSiteInfo.R`, `test_getDemographics.R`, `test_getFocalAnimalPed.R` (the `mockery` idiom). Constraints: (a) `getParamDef()` **stops** on an absent token → an optional `apiKey` needs a soft reader; (b) `test_getSiteInfo.R` asserts `getSiteInfo()`'s **exact** field-name vector → do NOT add `apiKey` to `getSiteInfo` (would break the test + ripple through ~10 Shiny-module callers). So the helper reads the token itself; `getSiteInfo` untouched (lowest blast radius).
  - **REAL WORK #2 — RED (7 + 3 failing tests, proven):** `tests/testthat/test_setLabKeyDefaults.R` (7 tests / 25 assertions: env-var key, config-token key, env-over-config precedence, netrc via `$NETRC`, netrc via home dir, no-credential `stop`, empty-string-env-as-absent) using `mockery` (stubs ONLY `labkey.setDefaults`) + `withr` (env control), all routed through an injected `siteInfo` so every branch is deterministic off host state. Added `getDemographics()` wiring tests (auto-auth + error propagation) to `test_getDemographics.R` + made its skip-heavy integration test tolerate the new fail-fast. Ran both → all fail (function undefined / wiring absent).
  - **REAL WORK #3 — GREEN (minimum impl):** `R/setLabKeyDefaults.R` (exported): apiKey from `Sys.getenv("NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY")` else `getConfigApiKey(siteInfo$configFile)`; if present → `Rlabkey::labkey.setDefaults(apiKey=, baseUrl=)` (returns `method="apiKey"`); else if `hasNetrc()` → `method="netrc"`; else `stop("No LabKey credential found." ...)`. Internal `R/getConfigApiKey.R` (soft token read) + `R/hasNetrc.R` (`$NETRC` then home `.netrc`/`_netrc`). Wired `R/getDemographics.R` to call `setLabKeyDefaults(siteInfo)` before `labkey.selectRows()`. `roxygenise()` → NAMESPACE export + `man/setLabKeyDefaults.Rd`. Full suite **0 failed / 0 error / 1926 passed / 167 skipped**.
  - **REAL WORK #4 — REFACTOR/finalize + verify:** diagnosed 4 `object_usage_linter` warnings as a **stale-install artifact** (only the NEW cross-refs flagged; 0 after `load_all`; CI installs `local::.` before linting — verified `lint.yaml`) → no `# nolint` on correct code (→ Learning 137). Docs: setup guide `docs/setup/labkey-authentication.md`; documented the optional `apiKey` token as a COMMENT in `inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config` (doc only — NOT the separate Rec #2 defaults-move); `NEWS.Rmd` "(development version)" entry + re-rendered `NEWS.md` (Learning 132 applied: error message → code span to dodge the pandoc smart-quote artifact; confirmed no smart quotes in the diff). `spell_check_package()` → 0 flags.

**Gotchas:** (1) **Two commits are now UNPUSHED:** S143's close-out `1a61dd4a` was already on local `master` unpushed at session start; S144's work is on a NEW local branch **`labkey-apikey-auth`** (off that `master` tip), also unpushed. `origin/master` is still at `659aa864` (S142). Publishing is the owner's call — push the feature branch + PR (the S142 convention; lets CI run), or fast-forward `master`. (2) **`getDemographics()` now FAILS FAST** without a credential — calls `setLabKeyDefaults()` which `stop()`s with "No LabKey credential found." if there is no `NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY` env var, no `apiKey` config token, and no netrc. This is intended; it replaces a later opaque `Rlabkey` error. Anything that drives `getDemographics`/`getLkDirectRelatives` for real now needs a credential (the test suite stubs them, so tests are unaffected). (3) **The auth path is NOT validated against a live LabKey server** (none available here) — the branch logic is fully mock-tested + runtime-smoked, but the actual server handshake is unexercised. (4) **`lintr::object_usage_linter` falsely flags freshly-added functions** as "no visible global function" until the package is re-installed/loaded — re-lint after `pkgload::load_all(".")` (→ Learning 137); CI is clean because `lint.yaml` installs `local::.`. (5) **`getSiteInfo()` was deliberately NOT given an `apiKey` field** (its exact field set is asserted by `test_getSiteInfo.R`); the credential reader is self-contained — keep it that way unless you also update that test + the Shiny callers. (6) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` — never commit. **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-19
**Status:** **DONE. Strict-TDD REFACTOR** (declared every response; behavior-neutral). **Phase gates:** one scope `AskUserQuestion` (3 owner decisions) + one REFACTOR phase-gate `AskUserQuestion` before any edit. **0 stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized **fan-out under ultracode** — the 46 per-file fixes ran through a fix->adversarial-review workflow (56 agents), but the load-bearing step was my **central re-verification** (re-lint + full suite + `R CMD check` + reading the entire diff), which caught a cascade the per-file fixers were blind to. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Two commits planned:** the content commit (config + 28 source + `man/`) + this close-out commit (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES).
  - **REAL WORK #1 — established the true baseline + the one real risk before editing:** local `lint_package()` = 57 (= CI exactly), `lintr`/`cyclocomp` installed locally so verification is firsthand. The "dragon": `coalesce_linter` (11) wants `%||%`, but it needs R >= 4.4 (pkg `Depends: R (>= 4.1.0)`) and shiny 1.13 **no longer exports** `%||%` (verified `"%||%" %in% getNamespaceExports("shiny")` = FALSE) — so adopting it would silently break R 4.1-4.3. Surfaced this; owner chose to **disable `coalesce_linter`** in `.lintr` (consistent with the 7 linters it already NULLs).
  - **REAL WORK #2 — fixed 46 code lints across 28 files via a fix->adversarial-review workflow:** 31 `return_linter` (drop terminal `return()`; restructure terminal `if/else` chains — chooseDate/removeDuplicates/convertDate/dataframe2string/qcStudbook), 6 `seq_along`, 5 `file.path` (1 R/ + 4 data-raw), 2 line wraps, 2 `!any`. 27 files clean by both fixer + independent reviewer.
  - **REAL WORK #3 — central re-lint caught a CASCADE the fixers couldn't see (-> Learning 134):** after the workflow, re-lint read **5, not 0** — all in `removeDuplicates.R`: the `boolean_arithmetic` autofix `sum(duplicated(x))==0L` -> `!any(duplicated(x))` cleared its target but TRIGGERED `if_not_else` + `any_duplicated` + `unnecessary_nesting`. Rewrote to `if (anyDuplicated(x) > 0L) <dups> else NULL` + a guard clause for the `stop()` branch -> all four cleared, behavior preserved (incl. empty-vector edge). Re-lint -> **0**.
  - **REAL WORK #4 — context-blind-linter judgments:** (a) `nonportable_path_linter` flagged a **MIME-type** string in `modPedigree`'s `fileInput(accept=...)`; the author had already `# nolint`-ed the adjacent `"text/csv"` line (intent = false positive) but missed the second string — the fixer wrapped the MIME type in `file.path()` (output identical, semantically wrong), so I restored the readable string + extended the `# nolint`. (b) `cyclocomp` wart: added `any::cyclocomp` to `lint.yaml` (`.lintr` already NULLs the linter; `linters_with_tags()` warns at config-load when absent — non-fatal). (c) the one roxygen line wrap (`R/data.R:358`) made `man/` stale, so regenerated (roxygen2 == pinned 8.0.0; diff confined to `man/rhesusPedigree.Rd`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED by `R CMD check`.** The change is behavior-neutral R code + lint/CI config; `R CMD check` (`--no-manual --ignore-vignettes`) loaded the namespace, ran all examples (+ donttest) and the full testthat + spelling suites -> **Status: OK (0/0/0)**, which exercises the real runtime paths. No separate Shiny-app launch needed: no startup/dispatch/config-resolution behavior changed (the only `mod*.R` edits were a `# nolint` comment + a restored-identical MIME string + implicit-return/`seq_along`/`%||%`-free rewrites). The `.lintr`/`lint.yaml` edits are CI-only, not runtime.

**Gotchas:** (1) **`lint` is now GREEN locally (0 lints) but the GitHub check is still RED on `master`** — the fix is on `add-methodology`, not yet merged; the `lint` workflow does NOT run on push to `add-methodology` (only `pull_request` + push to `main`/`master`), so it goes green when the next PR runs. (2) **`coalesce_linter` is permanently DISABLED in `.lintr`** by design — do NOT "re-enable + adopt `%||%`" without first bumping `Depends: R (>= 4.4.0)` or adding a dependency that provides `%||%` (shiny 1.13 does not export it). (3) **A lint sweep is iterative** — an autofix can clear its target and trigger other linters (Learning 134); always re-run `lint_package()` over the whole tree until 0, never trust per-file results. (4) **`modPedigree`'s `fileInput(accept=)` MIME strings carry `# nolint: nonportable_path_linter`** — they are MIME types, not paths; do not "fix" them. (5) **`man/rhesusPedigree.Rd` is generated** — the wrap came from `R/data.R`'s roxygen; edit the source + `roxygenise()`, never hand-edit the `.Rd`. (6) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` — owner's keep-call; never commit. (7) **Branch:** `add-methodology` is further ahead of `master` after this; per Learning 129, `git fetch` + `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/master...add-methodology` before acting on any branch-sync/merge prerequisite. (8) Carried CRAN gotchas: package **ARCHIVED** on CRAN 2025-07-29 (Learning 95); CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated.

**Status:** **DONE. VERIFICATION/docs phase** (declared every response — no production logic changed; no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR transition, so no phase-gate `AskUserQuestion`). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Owner explicitly **overrode "1 and done"** to batch S138's menu; executed in a safe order with a commit + a confined-diff check between the generated-file re-renders. Right-sized **SOLO** under ultracode — surgical doc edits + an outward-facing PR; a fan-out would risk conflicting edits to the same tree and adds no confidence over a firsthand confined-diff read. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Two commits:** `6be6a8aa` (the 3 content fixes) + the close-out commit (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES); both pushed.
  - **REAL WORK #1 — diagnosed the `names'` flag as a RENDER artifact, fixed at source (→ Learning 132):** S138 said "fix the quote (don't whitelist)" at `NEWS.md:51`. But `NEWS.Rmd:53` *already* used straight ASCII quotes — pandoc's `smart` extension curls `'row.names'` → `‘row.names’` at render, and the closing `'` against `names` makes the bogus token `names’`. So there was no source quote to "straighten." Wrapped the literal message in backticks in `NEWS.Rmd` (`` `duplicate 'row.names' are not allowed` `` — code spans are smart-quote-exempt and read as the verbatim message), re-rendered `NEWS.md` (github_document, `html_preview=FALSE`) → **exactly a one-line diff**; `spelling::spell_check_package()` → **0 flags** (was 1). Did NOT hand-edit `NEWS.md` (generated) and did NOT disable `smart` globally (would curl-strip every other quote in the 500-line file).
  - **REAL WORK #2 — mapped the README duplication before cutting:** the citation rendered twice — first from the shared child `vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd` (also consumed by `a3manual.Rmd` + `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd`), second from README.Rmd's own hardcoded block (lines 46-51). Removed **only the README.Rmd block** → README-only blast radius (manual/tutorial vignettes untouched), citation survives once in the Introduction. Re-rendered via `devtools::build_readme()`; `git diff README.md` = removed block + an incidental, correct `getVersion()` date refresh (`2026-06-17` → `2026-06-19`). Confirmed `grep "For more information"` README.md = 1 hit.
  - **REAL WORK #3 — plan §9 reconcile, evidence-based:** verified firsthand DESCRIPTION `2.0.0`, README.md "Version 2.0.0", CITATION.cff `version: 2.0.0`; NEWS re-renders; version-tests green (S138 0/0). Marked `docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md` §9 row 3 ✅ + added a Phase-3 STATUS block matching the Phase-4/5 pattern.
  - **REAL WORK #4 — PR #54 opened (open, not merged):** `git fetch` + `rev-list` confirmed `origin/add-methodology` == HEAD (`0/0`, no push-before-PR needed — Learning 129); `git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION` = `1.1.0.9000` (PR genuinely needed). ### What Session 138 Did
**Deliverable:** owner pick **(option 2 — the real fix for issue #53)** — rename the malformed sire column from `si.re` (period) / `si re` (space) to **`sire.id`** across the 6 example pedigree datasets + sweep dependent source/docs/tests, and remove the now-unneeded `si` `inst/WORDLIST` whitelist (S137's tentative resolution, **superseded**). **(DONE.)** Full **strict TDD** RED→GREEN→REFACTOR→VERIFY with a phase-gate `AskUserQuestion` at every transition. **Target name `sire.id` (owner-chosen):** a period in a column name is a realistic "inexperienced data provider" messy header, so the fix KEEPS a period-bearing header under test (rather than removing periods) — but in a sensible place that tokenizes to real words (`sire`+`id`) so it no longer spell-flags. Verified: `fixColumnNames("sire.id")` → `sire`, exercising MORE normalization than `si.re` (periodRemoved + sireIdToSire vs periodRemoved only).
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-19
**Status:** **DONE. Strict TDD** (declared every response). **Gated `AskUserQuestion`s (4):** PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR (all "full scope"), plus the pre-RED owner confirmation of the target name. **0 stakeholder corrections.** Builds directly on S137's audit (issue #53) — this is the deferred real fix the owner chose to do now instead of S137's whitelist. New session within the conversation (claimed it, 1B stub BEFORE technical work).
  - **RED:** new `tests/testthat/test_exampleData_columnNames.R` — (1) data contract pinning all 6 datasets' exact names to include `sire.id`; (2) malformed-name-absent check; (3) a normalization-invariant guard (`fixColumnNames` → canonical). Confirmed RED: tests 1+2 = 12 assertions FAIL (names held `si.re`/`si re`), guard = 6 PASS, 0 errors.
  - **GREEN (minimum to pass):** regenerated the 6 `.RData` — `load → rename the si.re/si re column to sire.id → save(compress="gzip")`, with a `stopifnot(identical(new, old))` proving **values byte-identical** (only the name moved); nrow preserved. **Status:** **DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response).** **Two gated `AskUserQuestion`s for the genuine fork + the mandatory transitions:** (1) pre-RED **approach** — rewrite the lead-in vs append a clause (owner chose **rewrite the lead-in**); (2) **PRE-RED→RED**; (3) **RED→GREEN**; (4) **GREEN→REFACTOR** declared **N/A** (prose content, structure preserved). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode: **SOLO** — a 3-line prose fix; the value is catching that the carried "omits sex" framing was imprecise, binding the RED probe to the live object, and rendering to verify the prose now matches the chunk. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work).
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #1 — THE CARRIED ONE-LINER WOULD HAVE PRODUCED A WRONG FIX (→ Learning 122):** the carried nit said "omits the `sex` column," which reads as "add `sex` to the parenthetical." A probe on the live object refuted that literal fix: `names(pedGood)` = `ego_id`, `si.re`, `dam_id`, **`sex`**, `birth_date`, and the article's own `changes` chunk renames exactly the FOUR non-`sex` columns — `sex` is **absent from `changedCols`** because it already uses the canonical name. Adding `sex` to the "deliberately messy headers" list would have mislabeled it AND contradicted the chunk's rendered output two lines below. The accurate fix conveys "five columns: four messy + an already-clean `sex`." (S116's richer framing "the dataset is 5 columns" was buried ~350 lines down in the notes; the compressed carried one-liner lost it.)
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #2 — BOUND THE RED PROBE TO THE LIVE OBJECT + SOURCE `.qmd` (Learning 122):** a vignette has no generated `man/.Rd` to bind to (unlike Learnings 119/120). The probe parses the lead-in paragraph from the `.qmd` and asserts 3 ground-truth **preconditions** (`names(pedGood)` has 5 cols incl. `sex`; the 4 listed headers == pedGood's non-`sex` columns) + 2 **RED assertions** (lead-in mentions `sex`; states "five") → **2 failures / 3 passing** on current prose; **0 failures (all 5 pass)** after the edit. Binding to `names(pedGood)` means the test fails if the data ever diverges from the prose.

### What Session 125 Did

### What Session 125 Did
**Deliverable:** owner's pick **(A9)** — hardened `addGenotype()` so its integer allele encoding is **consistent regardless of whether the two allele columns arrive as character or factor**. **(DONE — coerced both allele columns to character at the top of the function; factor input now produces output identical to character input, verified on the real bundled `rhesusGenotypes`: 35-allele dictionary codes 10001–10035, max distinct codes per allele = 1.)** This **realizes the latent code fragility S124 surfaced and flagged** (Learning 117): `genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]` / `[, 3L]` indexes a name-keyed integer vector by a factor → R uses the factor's INTEGER CODES not its labels → encoding inconsistent between the two columns (and between callers). First **CODE fix** in the S118–S125 run (S118–S122 were doc/prose; S123–S124 were DATA). Scoped to **`R/addGenotype.R` + `tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R` + `man/addGenotype.Rd`**; **no `NAMESPACE`/`DESCRIPTION`/`NEWS`/`data/`/`checkGenotypeFile.R` change** (`NAMESPACE` proven byte-identical).
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-17
**Status:** **DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response).** **Two gated `AskUserQuestion`s for the genuine forks + the 3 mandatory transitions:** (1) pre-RED **scope** — addGenotype-only vs also-harden-checkGenotypeFile (owner chose **addGenotype-only** / point-of-use); (2) **PRE-RED→RED**; (3) **RED→GREEN**; (4) **GREEN→REFACTOR**. The coerce-at-top-vs-coerce-at-index implementation detail was ground-truth-determined (coerce-at-top is strictly more robust — it also fixes the version-fragile `c()`-on-factors dict build) → presented as the gate baseline, NOT re-gated (Learning 113). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only **Explore breadth scan** to confirm scope (callers / sibling-pattern / checkGenotypeFile behavior) with **all load-bearing claims verified firsthand**, then **SOLO** TDD (the fix is a deterministic 2-line coercion — a fan-out for the edit would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work).
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #1 — THE SCOPE DECISION TURNED ON THE CALL GRAPH, NOT THE DOC (→ Learning 118):** `addGenotype`'s roxygen says genotype "is to be provided by `checkGenotypeFile` so it is not checked" — which reads as "harden the gate." The breadth scan + firsthand reads refuted that as the *complete* answer: `addGenotype` has **DIRECT callers that bypass `checkGenotypeFile`** (the roxygen example passing `rhesusGenotypes`, `test_addGenotype.R:17`, `test_geneDrop.R:28`), so a gate-only fix leaves them exposed. The only fix that closes the bug for ALL callers is at the point of use. Also confirmed: `checkGenotypeFile` is a pure structural validator (no type coercion), and **no sibling genotype function shares the `dict[factorColumn]` pattern** (`getGVGenotype`/`getGenotypes`/`getGenoDefinedParentGenotypes`/`hasGenotype` are clean) → scope stayed narrow.
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #2 — THE RED FIXTURE HAD TO MAKE PER-COLUMN CODES DIVERGE FROM THE GLOBAL SORT:** the naive fixture (both columns the same allele set in sorted order) does NOT reproduce the bug — per-column factor codes coincidentally align with the global `sort(unique())` positions. The minimal trigger is `first_name = c("a","b")`, `second_name = c("b","c")` as factors: globally a→10001, b→10002, c→10003, but the per-column codes (a,b→1,2 and b,c→1,2) make allele **"b" encode as 10002 in `first` but 10001 in `second`**. Designing it required tracing the exact index arithmetic, not just "make it a factor." Character input is the oracle.
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #3 — COERCE AT THE TOP, NOT ONLY AT THE INDEX:** the breadth agent suggested wrapping only the two lookups (`genoDict[as.character(genotype[, 2L])]`). That fixes the lookup but leaves the dict BUILD (`sort(unique(c(col1, col2)))`) relying on `c()`-on-factors (pre-R-4.1 dropped factor attrs → mis-named dict). Coercing both columns to character once at the top fixes build AND lookup in one place, version-robustly.
  - **RED:** two new tests in `test_addGenotype.R` — (1) factor-input output identical to character-input output (`expect_identical` on `$first` and `$second`); (2) allele "b" gets the same code as `first` and as `second` — confirmed **2 failed / 0 error** on the unhardened function; the existing character-input test stayed green (4/0). **GREEN:** the 2-line coercion at the top of `addGenotype`; all 3 tests pass; full suite 0/0. **REFACTOR:** added a one-line `@details` roxygen note documenting the internal coercion; regenerated `man/addGenotype.Rd` (rd roclet only → NAMESPACE untouched).
  - **Used file-based R probes from the first command** — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires.
  - **[news-vs-changelog]:** dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. This change **SHIPS** and is **user-facing** (`addGenotype` output changes for any factor-columned input) → fold into the **CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0** (flagged, NOT edited now). **NO auto-memory** — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** committed ONLY the repo files; excluded `.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep); the four `_s125_*.R` probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no `Rplots.pdf`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE — this IS a runtime-behavior change.** "Suite passes" was necessary but insufficient (the fix changes `addGenotype` output for factor input). Ran `addGenotype(rhesusPedigree, rhesusGenotypes)` on a **factor-coerced copy of the real bundled object** (375×12, 31 genotype rows): `first`/`second` came out **identical** to the character path; the combined dictionary is the full **35 alleles (codes 10001–10035)**; **max distinct codes per allele = 1** (consistent encoding). Computed the consistency property at the **genotype-row level** (`gr <- !is.na(first_name)`, aligned `al`/`co` vectors) to avoid the S124 short-logical-recycling trap (gotcha #11). Full `R CMD check` not run (`devtools` absent) — proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the factor-vs-character smoke on the real object.

**Session 124 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 125): Score 9/10.** S124 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as **(A9)** and made it trivial to execute: it gave the exact bug mechanism (`genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]` indexes by integer codes not labels), the exact fix location ("coerce the allele columns to character inside `addGenotype`"), the exact test design hint ("feed a factor-columned genotype, assert consistent name-based codes"), and the exact numbers to expect (combined dict 35 alleles, codes 10001–10035 vs the buggy per-column 18/23). Every carried gotcha held: the shell-backslash→file-probe trap (I used file probes from command #1, no misfires), the **consistency-check trap gotcha #11** (verify at the genotype-row level, NOT by indexing the 375-row output with a 31-row logical — S124 hit this itself and documented it; I followed the warning and avoided the round-trip), the standing `.DS_Store`/`PED_GV_AUDIT` keep-call, and the branch state (5 ahead of `origin/add-methodology`). ROI very high; no false credit; the bug characterization was accurate firsthand. **The −1:** S124's A9 wording offered the fix location as "inside `addGenotype` (or in `checkGenotypeFile`)" without noting that **direct callers bypass `checkGenotypeFile`**, so the "or checkGenotypeFile" branch — taken literally — would NOT have fully closed the bug. It listed `addGenotype` first (correct lead), but I had to run the breadth scan to confirm the gate-only path was insufficient. A perfect handoff would have flagged the direct-caller asymmetry.

claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Headline strengths:** (1) **the scope decision turned on the call graph, not the doc contract** — proved (via breadth scan + firsthand reads) that direct callers bypass `checkGenotypeFile`, so the point-of-use fix is the only complete one, and that the bug is isolated (no sibling pattern) so scope stayed narrow; (2) **designed a RED fixture that actually reproduces the bug** — traced the index arithmetic to make per-column codes diverge from the global sort (the naive fixture would have passed even unfixed); (3) **coerced at the source, not just the buggy line** — also neutralized the version-fragile `c()`-on-factors dict build the breadth agent's patch would have left; (4) **verified everything firsthand** (bug mechanism, caller types, checkGenotypeFile behavior) — used the Explore scan for completeness but re-derived its load-bearing claims; 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed all temp probes. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) full `R CMD check` NOT run (`devtools` absent) — the checkRd + load_all + suite + NAMESPACE-diff + smoke is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check; (b) low technical difficulty — the fix is 2 lines; the value is the scope analysis, the reproducing fixture, and the realistic smoke, not depth; (c) I did not add an explicit NA-with-factor RED case (the coercion is NA-safe — `as.character(factor-with-NA)` preserves NA, and the character NA path is already covered by `test_geneDrop.R` — but a dedicated assertion would have made it airtight; owner-approved RED scope was the two tests). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-`check`-verified.

  - **🚨 REAL WORK #1 — THE FIRSTHAND INVESTIGATION REFRAMED A7 (→ Learning 117):** S123 flagged A7 as "de-factor `id`". Probing showed **three** factor columns, and that `id` is the column that does NOT matter — `merge(ped, genotype, by="id")` coerces it either way and every consumer is id-agnostic (independently confirmed by the dependents scan). The columns that matter are the two **allele** columns: `addGenotype()`'s `genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]` indexes a name-keyed vector by a factor → R uses the factor's INTEGER CODES not its labels → an **inconsistent** encoding (same allele → different code in `first` vs `second`). Character columns index by name → **consistent** (verified: same allele→same code = TRUE for character / FALSE for factor; combined dict 35 alleles, codes 10001–10035 vs the buggy per-column 10001–10018 / 10001–10023). The package's own `test_addGenotype.R` already feeds `stringsAsFactors=FALSE` — the shipped factor object was the anomaly. So scope was a **correctness** decision, not cosmetic; gated it; owner chose all three.
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #2 — PROVENANCE confirmed COERCE-not-REGENERATE:** `data/rhesusGenotypes.RData` has a single 2020 commit (`31c4679d`), no scripted generator, shares all 31 obfuscated ids with `rhesusPedigree` (re-deriving via non-deterministic `obfuscatePed()` would change ids + desync the sibling). New idempotent `data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R` coerces the three columns and re-saves in place (`compress="xz"`, 734→608 B). The value-identical `inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv` is an independent cross-check (all 3 columns identical).
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #3 — BUNDLED doc + man with the data change:** reverted `R/data.R:347` id doc `factor`→`character` (first_name/second_name make no type claim, untouched) and regenerated `man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd` (1-line diff) in the SAME deliverable so the data-doc-mismatch class (S114–S122) stays closed.
  - **RED:** `test_rhesusGenotypes.R` (22 assertions pinning types AND value-preservation — dim 31×3, names, unique 31/18/23, NA 0, membership spot-checks 0BNY0G/I67LRJ/K0M2RD, A004_B002/A008_B015b, A004_B048a/A002a_B069a), confirmed **failing** on the factor object (the 6 `expect_type("character")`/`expect_false(is.factor())` assertions; value assertions already passed). **GREEN:** ran the coercion, the 1-line doc revert, regenerated `man/`. **REFACTOR:** N/A (own code clean; no redundant conversions introduced — no test references the bundled genotype object).
  - **Used file-based R probes from the first command** — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires.

**Status:** **DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response).** **Five gated `AskUserQuestion`s:** (1) pre-RED **scope** — Type-correctness vs full-canonical-match vs minimal (owner chose **Type-correctness**: fix the genuine type bugs, keep `sex` factor F,M and `gen` integer — no gratuitous widening); (2) pre-RED **reproducibility** — add a `data-raw/` script vs in-place-only (owner chose **add data-raw script**); (3) **PRE-RED→RED**; (4) **RED→GREEN**; (5) **GREEN→REFACTOR**. The 3 transition gates are the project's mandatory TDD contract; the 2 pre-RED questions were the genuine forks. The determined fixes (birth→Date, exit→Date, id/sire/dam→character — all ground-truth-determined) were the presented baseline, not re-gated (Learning 113). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only **4-prong investigation workflow** (provenance / target-structure / dependents / test-patterns) to frame the scope decision, then **SOLO** implementation + oracle verification (the work itself is a deterministic coercion — a fan-out would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work).
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #1 — PROVENANCE forced COERCE-not-REGENERATE (→ Learning 116):** the `.rda` has **no reproducible generator** — obfuscated from `inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` via `obfuscatePed()`, hand-saved 2020-02-02 (commit 31c4679d), never scripted/seeded. Obfuscation is non-deterministic, so re-deriving from the CSV would produce **different** obfuscated ids/dates than the shipped object (and desync sibling objects sharing those ids). The committed `data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R` therefore **loads the existing object and coerces its TYPES while preserving all VALUES** (idempotent), rather than regenerating. "Re-export" for an opaque artifact = value-preserving type coercion, not regeneration.
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #2 — INVESTIGATED BEFORE GATING to PRUNE UNSAFE OPTIONS (→ Learning 116):** two plausible-sounding options were eliminated before the owner ever saw them — "re-derive from CSV" (non-deterministic) and "drop `exit`" (`getPotentialParents.R:68-76,114` reads `ba$exit`; dropping breaks the object's main consumer). Verified firsthand that **no test asserts the current factor/logical types** (grep: none) so the fix breaks nothing. The scope gate offered only safe choices.
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #3 — BUNDLED the doc + man with the data change:** changing the object's types without updating its `R/data.R` doc + regenerating `man/` in the SAME session would have reopened the exact data-doc-mismatch class S114–S122 spent eight sessions closing. So one coherent deliverable: `.rda` + `data-raw/` + doc reversal (id `factor`→`character`; birth `factor of birth-date strings`→`Date vector`; exit `logical`→`Date`) + `man/` regen + tests + `.Rbuildignore`.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE — this IS a runtime-behavior change.** "Suite passes" was necessary but insufficient (the potential-parents tests do their own `as.character`/`as.Date`, now no-ops, which could mask a still-wrong type). (Note: `getPotentialParents()` returns an **unnamed positional list** — the tests index by position, e.g. `dams_1`=element 1, `dams_4`=element 4 — so `names(result)` are NOT focal ids; an early naive spot-check on that was reconciled, not a defect.) Full `R CMD check` not run (`devtools` absent) — proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all 162 + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the consumer smoke test.

"\\.Rd$" ...'` and `grep("^\\\\title", ...)` get mangled through the Bash layer (a backslash level stripped → `'\.' is an unrecognized escape` / a `\t` tab matching nothing). Switched to **file-based R probes** (Write tool, run `Rscript file.R`) + backslash-free constructs (`endsWith`, `[.]`, `fixed=TRUE`, `[[:space:]]`). An 8-agent workflow (verifier + adversarial cross-checker per claim) confirmed 3 and **refuted `getPyramidPlot`**: its `@return` ("the return value of `par('mar')`") is correct — the function returns `plotrix::pyramid.plot()`'s value, but `pyramid.plot` ends with `return(oldmar)` where `oldmar <- par("mar")`, so the returned value *is* a `par("mar")` vector (verified: returns `c(5.1,4.1,4.1,2.1)`). Trusting the flag would have replaced a correct `@return` with a wrong one. The cross-check also surfaced same-defect instances beyond scope (the sibling `p` descriptions; a 2nd `orderReport` bullet).
  - **🚨 REAL WORK #2 — THE roxygen VERSION-MISMATCH BLAST-RADIUS TRAP (→ Learning 106):** `roxygen2::roxygenise()` rewrote **~30** `.Rd` files + changed `DESCRIPTION` — far beyond my 4 functions — because committed `man/` is roxygen **7.3.2** (`RoxygenNote: 7.3.2`) but the dev lib is **8.0.0** (reformats every page; migrates the field to `Config/roxygen2/version:`). I `git checkout -- man/ DESCRIPTION` to drop the version artifacts, then hand-edited the 4 affected `\value` blocks to match source — scoping the diff to 5 `R/` + 4 `man/`. **General rule: read `git diff --stat` after any regen; a diff beyond your edits = a tool/version mismatch reformatting files — don't commit it as part of a content fix.**

(2) **That makes the example's apparent ~3:1 *female* skew REVERSED from reality:** the plot shows 35 F / 11 M, but the living colony is 35 F / **54 M** (male-majority). A plot from incomplete data can show the OPPOSITE of reality, not just an attenuated version — this became the article's central honesty / QC-tie-in lesson. (3) **Verified through 3 paths:** `quarto render` (Quarto 1.7.33) → clean HTML + both figures (deterministic *shape* — no RNG/seed; `qcPed` ships a fixed `age` col; only the title date drifts via `lubridate::now()`); `pkgdown::build_article("articles/age-sex-pyramid")` (pkgdown 2.2.0) wraps it; an `R CMD build` tarball confirms `vignettes/articles/` absent → zero CRAN risk, 21 shipping vignette files intact. This handed me the teaching example for free: rank-1 `1SPLS8` has higher genome uniqueness but NOT the lowest mean kinship — which is exactly why the ranking uses both metrics. (2) **Source-checked the scoring scheme in the implementation, not the roxygen** — `orderReport.R`/`rankSubjects.R` (the High-Value mean-kinship tier gates on the **z-score** `zScores <= 0.25`, not raw mean kinship) and `calcFEFG.R` (fe = 1/Σp², fg = 1/Σ(p²/r), r = mean founder alleles retained → fg ≤ fe; Lacy 1989). **The article is MORE correct than the package's own roxygen in 3 places** (the `orderReport` @return "mean kinship < 0.25"; `calcFEFG`'s description of `p`; `reportGV` @return "A dataframe" — it returns a list). (4) **Verified the verifier:** the adversarial reviewer returned **no must-fix**; I confirmed each of its 3 low-severity flags against the code (`meanKinship.R` is `colMeans` incl. the diagonal → "all animals incl. itself"; tier-2 sort ties break on ascending z-score; imports tier also requires `id %in% founders`), applied the fixes, and re-rendered (output unchanged — prose-only).

  - **What changed (1 doc converted + 5 housekeeping):** **`inst/extdata/meeting_notes.qmd`** — `git mv` from `.Rmd` (preserves history) + the single YAML line `output: html_document` → `format: html` (matching siblings `claude_code.qmd` / `software_design_doc.qmd`); **body byte-for-byte** (`git diff -M HEAD` = `similarity index 99%`, only the YAML line differs). Plus **`docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md`** (§7.1 slice-1 row → ✅ Done), **`ROADMAP.md`** (slice-1-done note under the doc-engine policy), **`CHANGELOG.md`** (S106 `[Unreleased]` entry), **`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`** (Learning 100), **`SESSION_NOTES.md`** (this handoff). **No `.Rbuildignore` edit needed** (see below). **No `R/`, test, NAMESPACE, `man/`, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, or vignette change.**
  - **🚨 THE REAL WORK WAS THE VERIFICATION (→ Learning 100) — a rename-only "conversion" would have hidden three things:** (1) **An Rmd→qmd SEMANTIC difference.** The render warned that `nprcgenekeepr:::` (R's internal-call operator, quoted from a 2020 CRAN-review reply, in **prose**) looked like a malformed `:::` fenced div — `:::` is meaningful in Quarto, inert in R Markdown. Checked the rendered HTML: it renders correctly (mid-line `:::` is not a div delimiter → heuristic false-positive, no output defect). **Left byte-faithful** rather than rewrite the author's historical prose for a cosmetic warning (FM #22). (2) **The doc is NOT reproducibly renderable — and that's not mine to "fix."** Its 5 R chunks hardcode 2020 absolute paths (`/Users/rmsharp/.../20160816_GeneticManagementTools`) and need packages absent from the default lib (standing renv-not-materialized); `include=FALSE` hides output but the chunk still *executes*, so a full render fails on the dead paths. It's a **historical meeting-notes log**, not a live computational doc. Build-equivalent satisfied via `quarto render --no-execute` (YAML + structure → 81 KB HTML, code shown not run); execution limitation **stated, not silently skipped**. Making it executable would be a behavior change (FM #8). (3) **"It was build-ignored" ≠ "the new file is."** Confirmed by applying every `.Rbuildignore` regex to BOTH filenames in R: `meeting_notes.Rmd` and `meeting_notes.qmd` both `ships=FALSE` (covered by `^inst/extdata/meeting_notes\.` — extension-agnostic — and `^inst/extdata/.*\.qmd$`). Had the pattern been `...\.Rmd$`, the conversion would have started SHIPPING the `.qmd` into the CRAN tarball — a silent package-contents change for an archived package mid-resubmission.
  - **[news-vs-changelog]:** a build-ignored dev-doc format standardization = dev-process history → **CHANGELOG only** (S106 entry), no NEWS (never user-facing). **NO auto-memory** — the decision/slice now lives in the repo (analysis §7.1 + ROADMAP + CHANGELOG) ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** committed ONLY the 6 changed files; excluded `.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep).

  - **All 6 load-bearing claims survived adversarial refutation at HIGH confidence:** (1) R Markdown is NOT deprecated — `rmarkdown` 2.31 (2026-03)/`knitr` 1.51 (2025-12) actively maintained CRAN-critical infrastructure (Posit: "not going away, no plans for deprecation"; Xie laid off 2023-12-31 but Posit-funded contractor to keep maintaining knitr/rmarkdown); the only cost of staying is feature stagnation, not breakage. (2) Quarto CRAN vignette adds a `SystemRequirements` Quarto-CLI dep NOT guaranteed on CRAN machines (missing on macOS flavors 2025) + a documented transient "no vignettes" NOTE — bad for an archived package; maintainer cderv "would not advise" it for CRAN. (3) Realized Quarto benefit for simple single-language HTML vignettes is narrow (CRAN engine is `theme:none`/`minimal:true` → callouts/tabsets/Bootstrap/multi-language disabled; only cross-references land). (4) Conversion can't fix timing (same knitr R-chunk cost + fixed overhead → slightly slower). (5) Precompute on the existing engine solves timing without Quarto. (6) Migration is mechanical/reversible; pkgdown supports mixed `.qmd`/`.Rmd` (2.1.0, 2024-07).
  - **Firsthand inventory (not just the audit agent):** all 4 CRAN vignettes use knitr engines (`knitr::rmarkdown_notangle` ×3; `a3manual` = `knitr::knitr`); `ColonyManagerTutorial` is `eval=FALSE` (screenshots only); `a2interactive` runs real analysis; `simulatedKValues` has 3 sims n=10/100/1000 with `set.seed(1L)` before each (deterministic → precompute-safe); `a3manual` is a 57-line wrapper over 13 `manual_components/_*.Rmd`. `VignetteBuilder: knitr`, no Quarto dep. The `vignettes/*.html|.R|.md` are git-ignored stale renders — **no precompute pattern exists**.
  - **[news-vs-changelog]:** an analysis/planning doc = dev-process history → **CHANGELOG only** (S104 entry), no NEWS ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], S101 plan precedent). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** commit ONLY the new analysis doc + `CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (L98) + `SESSION_NOTES.md`; exclude `.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep).

**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-17
**Status:** **DONE (Phase 2a). TDD phase = REFACTOR/mechanical** — every edit is no-numeric-change (skip guards change only WHAT runs on CRAN; native pipe is syntactic), so the plan's RED-first simulation-number rule did NOT apply (avoided, not triggered; declared every response). **Two `AskUserQuestion` gates:** a pre-RED **scope** decision (defer vignettes 2b; convert ALL `%>%`) + the **PRE-RED→REFACTOR** gate spelling out every edit + verification. **Owner steered live** (build-vs-run deps; `Config/Needs/build`; native `|>` instead of declaring magrittr; NEWS entry; the harness-misattribution catch; the jargon flag). Right-sized **SOLO** — measurement is CPU-sensitive (parallel checks would corrupt timing) and the fix is gated mechanical edits; a Workflow would corrupt the measurement and add no value.
  - **Measure-first profile (the cause, named by data not assumption — overturned the plan's single-suspect framing):** **examples** 6.6s total, slowest `countLoops` 1.28s — none >5s, none flag (no action). **Tests:** the CRAN-running slow ones are the **shiny module `testServer` tests** (`modGeneticValue` 4.4s / `modBreedingGroups` 4.6s / `modInput` 2.7s / `modBreedingGroups_groupAddAssign` 2.2s / `modPedigree_processing` 1.2s; `skip_if_not_installed("shiny")` only — shiny is an Import, so they RUN). The raw-slowest files (`fillGroupMembers*`/`groupAddAssign`, ~17s) are `skip_if_not(user=="rmsharp")` → owner-machine-only, **never on CRAN**. **Vignettes** ~21s (`ColonyManagerTutorial` 7.8s / `a2interactive` 7.5s / `simulatedKValues` 5.4s) → **Phase 2b** (the only iteration-reduction lever — `simulatedKValues` n=1000 gene-drop, 3.68s — would change displayed kinship numbers = a correctness regression, so it needs numeric-preserving precompute, a separate effort).
  - **What changed (13 files):** **Tests** — file-level `testthat::skip_on_cran()` added to `test_modGeneticValue.R`, `test_modBreedingGroups.R`, `test_modBreedingGroups_groupAddAssign.R`, `test_modInput.R`, `test_modPedigree_processing.R` (removes ~15s CRAN time; CI/local unaffected). **Native pipe** `%>%`→`|>` — `vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd` (5; dropped `library(magrittr)`), `vignettes/ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` (3), `tests/testthat/test_makeRelationsClasses.R` (2), `R/makeRelationClassesTable.R` `@examples` (2) + hand-synced `man/makeRelationClassesTable.Rd` (`\%>\%`→`|>`). **NEWS** — `NEWS.Rmd` Minor "Code modernization" bullet + re-rendered `NEWS.md`. Plus `CHANGELOG.md` (S103 entry), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (Learning 97), this handoff.
  - **Verification:** full core suite via `pkgload::load_all` at **`NOT_CRAN=true` → 169 files, 0 failed, 0 errors** (after installing the missing `shinyBS` Suggest in the dev lib — the earlier "52/69 errors" were 100% environment/harness, NOT regressions); at **`NOT_CRAN=false` the 5 mod files skip** (affected-file time 14.82s→0.01s). `simulatedKValues.Rmd` renders with native pipe (no magrittr); `makeRelationClassesTable` example runs; `man/*.Rd` parses; `NEWS.md` re-rendered with only the new bullet (no reformat); 0 `%>%` remain in any converted file.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not skipped.** No `R/` function BODY or app/runtime path changed (the `makeRelationClassesTable` edit is roxygen-comment-only; skip guards are test infra; NEWS is prose). The code that DID change is executed in vignettes/examples/tests and was run-verified (render + example + 0/0 suite). NOT FM #24 — there is no untested runtime behavior masked by a passing build; I verified the actual executed surface.

ROI high. **The −1:** two inherited assumptions were stale/incomplete — (a) the "renv NOT materialized, `restore()` required" gotcha: `renv::restore()` reported **already-synchronized** (the library lives at `~/Library/Caches/.../renv/library/nprcgenekeepr-229b003c/...`, Imports present); the real gap is that the **explicit snapshot omits ALL Suggests**, so Suggests (testthat, shinyBS, …) need separate installs — a different problem than "restore first"; (b) neither the plan nor the handoff flagged the `skip_if_not(user=="rmsharp")` test guards (which make raw timing misleading) or that the package's test harness needs `load_all` (not `library()`) to expose internals. Two genuine stakeholder corrections (vs the recent 0-correction streak). I reserve 9–10 for a clean-harness, zero-correction delivery.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (2025-07-29)** — any CRAN session is an archived-resubmission, not a routine update; the timing root cause must be MEASURED (profile examples/tests/vignettes under `--as-cran`), not assumed; `cran-comments.md` must address the archival (Learning 95). (2) **`NEWS.md` is generated from `NEWS.Rmd`** — edit the `.Rmd` (plain ASCII) and re-render; never edit `NEWS.md` directly. (3) **The 1.1.0.9000 NEWS section double-counts changes** — the rewrite must merge duplicates (plan §6.3 / dragon #4). (4) **`.Rbuildignore` is perl-regex — an unbalanced paren even in a comment aborts `R CMD build`** (anchored, paren-free lines only). (5) **renv library NOT materialized** (Learning 92) — `renv::restore()` is a prerequisite for the check phases. (6) **A speed-up that changes simulation/sampling numbers is a correctness regression** — RED-first (dragon #2). (7) **No reverse dependencies** — do NOT run `revdepcheck` (plan §3.5). (8) **The owner performs the CRAN upload + email confirmation** (Phase 5 STOP — outward-facing). (9) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner's standing keep-call). (10) **This close-out leaves `add-methodology` 1 ahead of origin** — push when ready (accumulate-then-push; S96/S99 pattern).

(2) `git fetch origin master add-methodology`. (3) The divergence looked alarming but was benign — `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/master...origin/add-methodology` = **2 / 54**, where the "2 ahead" on master are exactly the prior **`Merge pull request #43/#41`** nodes (`git log origin/add-methodology..origin/master` → `c6ad23dd`, `0363ffe3`), i.e. add-methodology's own work merged back, NOT independent master work. (4) **Three-part safety proof (→ Learning 94):** `git merge-tree --write-tree origin/master origin/add-methodology` → **exit 0, no conflicts**; `comm -23` of the two file trees → **no master-only files**; the tip-to-tip diff is an exact **mirror** (`master`-vs-add-methodology = `+406 / −6001`, the inverse of add-methodology's `+6001 / −406` over 114 files) → master is **strictly behind**, zero unique content. ⇒ merge gains exactly the 54 commits (S52–S99), loses nothing.
  - **PR body grounded in canonical sources, not commit-message memory:** read `NEWS.md` (1.1.0.9000) + the `CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased]` section for the user-facing summary; categorized the 54 commits (6 `feat` / 11 `fix` / 3 `test` / 33 `docs`). One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. **#33 left OPEN pending owner confirmation.**
  - **Recompute-don't-inherit (applied to the issue's claim):** verified FIRSTHAND that the issue's suggested `@return` is accurate before writing it — `R/fillBins.R:32` returns `list(males = maleBins, females = femaleBins)`; `maleBins`/`femaleBins` are built from `integer(0L)` + `nrow(...)` (→ integer vectors, one element per `seq_along(lowerAges)` bin); the existing values test confirms integer (`c(0L,0L,2L,1L,0L)`); the caller `R/getMaxAx.R:18` consumes them as `bins$male`/`bins$female` (R `$` partial-match of the plural names) = per-bin counts. ✓
  - **RED design (the load-bearing craft):** since `fillBins` is `@noRd` there is **no `man/fillBins.Rd`**, so S87's `tools::Rd2ex(man/<fn>.Rd)` pattern (`test_examples_invoke_documented_fn.R`) is UNAVAILABLE — the doc lives only in the SOURCE file, read via `test_path` + `skip_if`-installed (RED in dev, inert under `R CMD check` where `R/*.R` isn't shipped). **The false-pass trap I scoped around:** `fillBins.R`'s TITLE already says `list of two lists \code{males} and \code{females}`, so a whole-file `grepl("males", ...)` would be GREEN with the TODO still present — the test extracts ONLY the `@return` block (from the `@return` line to the next `#' @` tag) and uses `\bword\b` boundaries (so the `males` inside `females` doesn't satisfy the `males` assertion). All 3 assertions RED at HEAD for the right reason; the RED failure output literally printed the isolated `@return` line, proving the extractor scoped correctly.
  - **GREEN:** the issue's verified `\describe{\item{males}{...}\item{females}{...}}` wording (matches the package's existing `@return` convention — 4 files use `\describe`, e.g. `R/getPossibleCols.R`), all lines ≤80. **REFACTOR = confirmed no-op** (idiomatic, lint 0, lines ≤80).
  - **[news-vs-changelog]:** a `@noRd` internal's docs never render to a user-facing man page → **CHANGELOG only** (S92 entry), no `NEWS`. **[macos-dupe-scan]:** commit only the doc/test/notes files; exclude `.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

### What Session 90 Did
**Deliverable:** Finalize **#4** — CLOSED the issue (owner-confirmed; S89's fix `8a3e3631` was Phase-3E-verified) **and** added behavioral regression coverage for the Shiny **upload path** that S89 had covered only *structurally* (a `deparse(body(modInputServer))` grep). **(DONE — two NEW test files, test-only; no `R/`/NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION change. (1) `tests/testthat/test_modInput_incomplete_final_line.R` — in-process `shiny::testServer` driving `modInputServer`'s `getData` with a TINY no-trailing-newline upload through read.csv + read.table; asserts the warning does NOT escape + all 3 records survive QC; FIX-SPECIFIC teeth PROVEN by un-muffle round-trip (both blocks RED un-muffled, green restored). (2) `tests/testthat/test-e2e-input-incomplete-final-line.R` — opt-in `shinytest2` browser E2E uploading a no-trailing-newline `ExamplePedigree.csv` through both reader paths; asserts the cleaned studbook flows end-to-end to all 3,694 records in the Pedigree Browser. testServer 6/6, browser E2E 2/2, full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error, lines ≤80.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-16
**Status:** **DONE.** Development workstream (regression backfill of already-shipped behavior) under **Strict TDD**. TDD phase declared every response; gates via `AskUserQuestion`: a separate pre-RED **scope** question (both reader paths) + **PRE-RED→RED**, **RED→GREEN**, **GREEN→REFACTOR**, **+ a mid-GREEN reframe** question when the investigation changed what was testable; **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. **#4 CLOSED.**
  - **#4 closed first** (owner gave the confirmation S89 deferred): the fix shipped + Phase-3E-verified in S89; closed with an evidence comment referencing `8a3e3631`, the root cause, and the surgical muffle.
  - **THE load-bearing discovery (cost ~6 browser diagnostics + several install/`load_all` round-trips — see Learning 88):** `read.table`/`read.csv` emit `"incomplete final line found by readTableHeader"` **only for SMALL files** — the header/type-detection scan must actually REACH the unterminated final line; a realistic-size file (3694-row `ExamplePedigree`) **never triggers it**. AND the warning is a **console-only artifact**: in the assembled app it never reaches the DOM or `app$get_logs()`. ⇒ a browser `get_logs` warning-absence assertion is a TAUTOLOGY (it stayed green even un-muffled — the un-muffle round-trip exposed it). **Root self-critique:** I chased "the app swallows the warning" for many cycles before validating the FOUNDATIONAL assumption (does my fixture even reproduce the warning in a plain `read.csv`? It didn't). De-risk the FIXTURE, not just the observation mechanism.
  - **Resulting honest division of labor (surfaced to the owner via the mid-GREEN reframe `AskUserQuestion`):** the warning-suppression teeth live in the **in-process `testServer`** test (TINY 4-line fixture, where the warning IS observable via `withCallingHandlers`); the **browser E2E** owns end-to-end **data integrity** (a no-trailing-newline upload processes correctly to all records). Both reader paths covered in each.
  - **testServer test (normal suite):** drives `getData` with a `data.frame(name,size,type,datapath)` fileInput value + `session$setInputs(getData=1)` wrapped in `withCallingHandlers`; asserts `!any(grepl("incomplete final line", captured))` and `qcSummary()$records == 3`, `$errors == 0`, for read.csv (`fileTypeExcel`) and read.table (`fileTypeText`). **Teeth round-trip:** un-muffle BOTH `modInput.R` read sites → `load_all` → both blocks RED (`warned=TRUE`); `git checkout R/modInput.R` → 6/6 green.
  - **browser E2E (opt-in `NPRC_RUN_E2E`):** after upload+`wait_for_module_ready`, `navigate_to_tab(app, "Pedigree Browser")` → `#pedigree-pedigreeTable` shows `of 3,694 entries` (the modInput QC outputs are in SUSPENDED hidden nested tabs and don't reliably un-suspend; a top-level consumer is the robust signal — Learning 88).
  - **Verification:** testServer **6/6**; browser E2E **2/2** (real Chrome, INSTALLED app); full clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error** (195 files; 5 warnings = the designed `loadSiteConfig` safety-net logs); all test lines ≤80 (REFACTOR reflowed two 81–82-char lines). **[news-vs-changelog]:** test-only (the user-facing fix landed in NEWS at S89) → **CHANGELOG only** (S90 entry). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** commit only the 2 test files + docs; exclude `.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS.** The browser E2E IS the runtime verification (drove the INSTALLED app via real Chrome, both reader paths, end-to-end to the Pedigree Browser); the testServer test drives the real `modInputServer` reactive graph in-process. FM #24 answered — did not treat compile/lint-clean as verification.

Small TDD/test-only."* — which (with candidate (1), close #4) is exactly what the owner picked. Load-bearing elements that held firsthand: the precise gap (*the modInput upload path `readDataFile` is a server-internal closure covered only structurally — gotcha #5*), the candidate to *close #4 on confirmation*, and every baseline (dashboard 98/100, ghost-check → `8a3e3631`=S89, the `gh issue view --json` gotcha, the 2-untracked-files disposition, the close-out-on-`add-methodology` branch model, the standing "don't close #1/#5/#9/#45/#28-gated without owner evidence" list). **The −2:** S89 framed it as *"Small TDD/test-only,"* which badly mis-sized it — the muffle is **not browser-observable** and the warning **only fires for small files**, two facts that made a naive "upload a file, assert no warning in the browser" approach a tautology and forced the testServer pivot + the tiny-fixture discovery. Used `AskUserQuestion` for the pre-RED scope (both reader paths) + all three TDD gates + an honest **mid-GREEN reframe** when the discovery changed what was testable — declared the phase every response; **0 stakeholder corrections**. **The strength = honest teeth:** the un-muffle teeth round-trip caught a TOOTHLESS browser assertion before it shipped (it stayed green un-muffled), and I refused to ship a test claiming warning-teeth it didn't have — I surfaced the finding and split the deliverable into a testServer test that PROVABLY catches the regression (both blocks RED un-muffled) + a browser E2E that owns end-to-end. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (a contained two-file backfill with interactive gates; a Workflow would be theater AND can't pause for the gates). **The −2 (honest):** I burned many cycles on a wrong hypothesis ("the assembled app swallows the deferred warning") before checking the cheapest foundational thing — *does my chosen fixture reproduce the warning in a plain `read.csv`?* (it didn't; the file was too large). A single plain-R fixture check up front would have saved most of the investigation. That self-critique IS Learning 88: **de-risk the fixture, not just the observation mechanism; a teeth round-trip that won't go RED means a toothless assertion OR a non-triggering fixture — check the fixture first.**

**⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner's pick.** #4 finalized (closed + behaviorally covered); nothing mid-flight. **16 open issues** (#4 closed this session). Candidates:
- **(1) Push** `add-methodology` to origin (now ahead of origin by several commits after this close-out; S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).
- **(2) Open issues** #33 (complete `fillBins` `@return` docs — small docs TDD), #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid settings — overlaps open #46), #29 (rename `makeGrpNum`→`makeGroupNum` — a rename → run a cross-reference **grep inventory FIRST** per the SESSION_RUNNER planning rules + SAFEGUARDS rename caution).
- **(3) #37** (exported functions not used by the app) — an audit-shaped candidate.
**Do NOT** implement #28 (still gated on #11/#12/#37/#46), do NOT close #45, and do NOT close #1, #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence.

**Key files (this session):** **NEW (committed):** `tests/testthat/test_modInput_incomplete_final_line.R` (testServer warning-suppression teeth + data integrity; local `tiny_no_final_newline()` + `run_upload()` helpers), `tests/testthat/test-e2e-input-incomplete-final-line.R` (opt-in browser E2E, both reader paths → Pedigree Browser count; local `write_without_final_newline()`). **CHANGED (docs):** `CHANGELOG.md` (S90 entry), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (Learning 88 at tail), `SESSION_NOTES.md` (this handoff). **Read firsthand:** `R/modInput.R` (the `modInputUI` controls — `fileType` default `fileTypeExcel`, `fileContent` default `pedFile`→`pedigreeFileOne`, separator default `,`; the `getData` observer; `readDataFile` with the two muffled read sites at `:274` read.table / `:281` read.csv; the `qcSummaryUI`/`cleanedDataTable`/`downloadCleaned` outputs in nested tabs; the module return `cleanedStudbook`/`qcSummary`), `tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R` (`create_test_app` opt-in gate, `create_app_driver`, `upload_and_wait`, `wait_for_module_ready`, `navigate_to_tab`, `get_html_safe`, `E2E_TIMEOUT`), `tests/testthat/test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R` (the sibling pattern: `navigate_to_tab("Pedigree Browser")`, `of %d entries`), `R/appServer.R:38-43` (the `flog.logger` **file** appender — why `flog.debug` does NOT reach `get_logs`). **Transient (NOT committed):** `/tmp/s90_derisk/`, `/tmp/s90_derisk_tc/`, `/tmp/s90_diag*.R`, `/tmp/s90_ts.R`, `/tmp/s90_tiny.R`.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The `"incomplete final line"` warning fires ONLY for SMALL files** (≤ ~5 lines — the `readTableHeader` scan must reach the unterminated final line) AND is a **console-only artifact** (never in the DOM or `app$get_logs()` from the app). ⇒ it canNOT be teeth-tested in a browser; a browser `get_logs` absence assertion is a TAUTOLOGY. The suppression teeth live in the **testServer** test (TINY fixture). (2) **modInput's QC outputs are in SUSPENDED hidden nested tabs** (`dataInput-mainTabs`; `suspendWhenHidden=TRUE`) — `qcSummaryUI`/`cleanedDataTable`/`downloadCleaned` are empty/`disabled` until their tab is active, and neither `set_inputs(mainTabs=...)` nor a JS anchor-click reliably un-suspends them (bslib BS4 + broken shinyBS JS). Read counts via a **top-level consumer**: `#pedigree-pedigreeTable` in the Pedigree Browser (DataTables formats the count WITH a comma → match `"of 3,694 entries"`, not `3694`). (3) **`data-ready=true` is set server-side** (in the `getData` observer) and does NOT imply the hidden-tab outputs rendered or that `cleaned` is non-NULL. (4) **Teeth round-trip recipe (testServer):** un-muffle BOTH `modInput.R` read sites → `pkgload::load_all` → run the testServer file → both blocks RED → `git checkout R/modInput.R` → green. **No install needed for testServer**; the browser E2E needs `devtools::install()` first (drives the INSTALLED app). (5) **The browser E2E uses the BIG fixture** (3694 rows; warning never fires) = a pure end-to-end data-integrity net; the **testServer uses TINY fixtures** (warning fires) = the suppression teeth. Two complementary vehicles BY DESIGN — don't "consolidate" them. (6) **`flog.*` writes to a FILE appender** (`~/nprcgenekeepr.log` via `appServer.R:38-43`), NOT stderr — so `flog.debug("File read successfully. Rows: N")` does NOT reach `app$get_logs()`. (7) **#4 is CLOSED.** (8) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner's standing keep-call). (9) **Default `gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use `gh issue view N --json <fields>`. (10) **Installed package = muffled HEAD** (reinstalled at the end of this session). **Branch model unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

### What Session 89 Did
**Deliverable:** Fix **#4** — reading an animal list or pedigree file whose final line lacks a trailing newline emitted `"incomplete final line found by readTableHeader on '...'"` (reported from a Shiny text upload, `0.txt`). **(DONE — NEW non-exported `@noRd` helper `muffleIncompleteFinalLine()` muffles ONLY that warning; wrapped all 4 read sites; NEW `tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` 7/7 RED→GREEN; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error; lint 0 on the 5 changed `R/` files; no NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION change; Phase-3E on the INSTALLED app confirms the readers no longer emit the warning, rows preserved, and the muffle is surgical.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-16
**Status:** **DONE.** Development workstream under **Strict TDD**. TDD phase declared every response; **3 phase gates** via `AskUserQuestion` (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR) **+ a separate pre-RED scope question** (all-readers-via-helper vs named-readers vs app-only); **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out commit on `add-methodology`.
  - **Reproduce-first (recompute-don't-inherit, applied to the bug):** before designing anything, wrote a no-trailing-newline CSV and read it — confirmed the warning is **noise, not data loss** (`read.table`/`read.csv` read every row including the last; `nrow == 3`). That decided the fix shape: **suppress the warning**, not recover a row. Also confirmed `grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed = TRUE)` matches, so the muffle can target exactly this warning.
  - **Owner scope decision (separate pre-RED `AskUserQuestion`): All readers, shared helper** — root-cause over symptom-only. The same bug exists identically at 4 sites (`getPedigree.R:30` / `getGenotypes.R:30` `read.table`; `getFocalAnimalPed.R:44` `read.csv`; `modInput.R:274`+`:279` upload `readDataFile`). The mechanism (surgical `withCallingHandlers` muffle) was settled by reproduction, so only scope was the owner's fork.
  - **RED:** NEW `tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` (7 tests / 12 expectations). Helper teeth: no-newline read → no warning + all rows; an unrelated `warning("...")` **still propagates** (surgical, not blanket `suppressWarnings`); returns `expr` unchanged. Integration: `getPedigree`/`getGenotypes` → no warning + rows preserved; `getFocalAnimalPed` → no `"incomplete final line"` warning (its post-read LabKey call returns an errorLst without a DB — the unrelated config-missing warning is acceptable, so I assert absence of the SPECIFIC warning); `modInputServer` body references the helper (structural lock for the server-internal `readDataFile` closure). All 7 RED at HEAD for the right reason (helper missing → 3 errors `object 'muffleIncompleteFinalLine' not found`; readers warn → fail; body lacks wrap → fail). The 2 passing expectations at HEAD are the `nrow == 3` data-preservation guards (data was never lost).
  - **GREEN:** NEW `R/muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` — non-exported `@noRd` internal helper (matches `getRecordStatusIndex`/`readExcelPOSIXToCharacter` convention) = `withCallingHandlers(expr, warning = \(w) if (grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed=TRUE)) invokeRestart("muffleWarning"))`. No new imports (base fns). Wrapped each read: `x <- muffleIncompleteFinalLine(<existing read call>)` at all 4 sites. `document()` → **no** NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION change (`@noRd`; reader signatures unchanged — `git status`-confirmed). **REFACTOR = confirmed no-op** (helper + wraps minimal/idiomatic/lint-clean; one roxygen line-length reflow done in GREEN to reach lint 0).
  - **Verification:** new file **7/7 green** (12 expectations); full clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error** (192 files / 1046 tests / 167 skips; the 5 warnings are S85's designed `loadSiteConfig` safety-net `flog.warn` logs — confirmed by the "Could not find center" text); `lintr` **0 lints** on all 5 changed `R/` files.
  - **[news-vs-changelog]:** user-facing file-reading bug fix → **BOTH** `NEWS.Rmd`→`NEWS.md` (new bullet under `1.1.0.9000` → Data input / quality control; rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY the new bullet — pandoc's straight→curly-quote + `--`→en-dash conversion is expected, not churn) **AND** `CHANGELOG.md` (S89 entry). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** `git add` only source/test/doc/notes; excluded `.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS.** `devtools::install(quick=TRUE)` then exercised the exported readers in a fresh session with `library(nprcgenekeepr)` (the **INSTALLED** package, loaded from the renv library cache, NOT `load_all`): `getPedigree`/`getGenotypes` on no-trailing-newline files → **no** incomplete-final-line warning, all 3 rows preserved; `getFocalAnimalPed` → incomplete-final-line warning gone **but the unrelated "configuration file is missing" warning STILL propagates** (the surgical proof — the muffle is not a blanket suppression); a control file WITH a trailing newline still reads cleanly (no regression). The Shiny upload path (`readDataFile`) is a server-internal closure — covered by the structural test; a full `shinytest2` file-upload E2E is the heavier alternative (not done — stated, not silently skipped). FM #24 answered: did not treat lint/compile-clean as verification — ran the installed readers.

**Key files (this session):** **NEW (committed):** `R/muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` (non-exported `@noRd` helper), `tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` (7 tests / 12 expectations; local `write_no_final_newline()` + `captured_warnings()` helpers). **CHANGED (code):** `R/getPedigree.R` (`:30` `read.table` wrapped), `R/getGenotypes.R` (`:30` `read.table` wrapped), `R/getFocalAnimalPed.R` (`:44` `read.csv` wrapped), `R/modInput.R` (`readDataFile` text `read.table` `:274` + CSV `read.csv` `:279` wrapped). **CHANGED (docs):** `NEWS.Rmd`→`NEWS.md` (Data-input bullet), `CHANGELOG.md` (S89 entry), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (Learning 87 at tail), `SESSION_NOTES.md` (this handoff). **Read firsthand:** all 4 read-site functions; `R/getRecordStatusIndex.R` + `R/readExcelPOSIXToCharacter.R` (the `@noRd` internal-helper convention I matched); NAMESPACE (confirmed `modInputServer` exported, the 3 readers exported). **Transient (NOT committed):** `/tmp/s89_probe.R`, `/tmp/s89_red_probe.R`, `/tmp/s89_phase3e.R`, `/tmp/news_before_s89.md`.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The "incomplete final line" warning was NOISE, not data loss** — `read.table`/`read.csv` always read the last row correctly; the fix only suppresses the warning. Do not "fix" it by appending newlines / normalizing files. (2) **`muffleIncompleteFinalLine()` is surgical** — it muffles ONLY warnings whose message contains `"incomplete final line"`; every other warning still propagates (a malformed-file warning, the LabKey config-missing warning, etc.). Do NOT replace it with `suppressWarnings()`. (3) **The helper is `@noRd` (non-exported)** — reference it in tests as `nprcgenekeepr:::muffleIncompleteFinalLine`; `document()` does NOT generate a `man/*.Rd` or a NAMESPACE entry for it (the package's convention for internals). (4) **`getFocalAnimalPed` on a no-DB machine emits TWO warnings** (incomplete-final-line + "configuration file is missing"); assert absence of the SPECIFIC incomplete-final-line warning, not "no warnings". (5) **The `modInput` upload path (`readDataFile`) is a server-internal closure** — tested STRUCTURALLY (`deparse(body(modInputServer))` grep), not behaviorally; a `shinytest2` upload E2E is the heavier behavioral alternative if desired. (6) **#4 is left OPEN** pending owner confirmation. (7) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner's standing keep-call). (8) **Default `gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use `gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

**Gotchas:** (1) **The example-invokes-its-own-fn test reads `man/*.Rd` via `tools::Rd2ex`** — it RUNS under `load_all`/dev (the project's standard test invocation) and SKIPS under an installed package (no `man/`). So it does NOT protect under `R CMD check`'s installed-pkg test run; the example correctness there is enforced by `R CMD check`'s own example runner. (2) **For a roxygen-example fix, "example runs without error" is a WEAK test** — broken examples often still run (`getPedDirectRelatives`'s buggy one soft-failed to `NULL`); the discriminating test is STRUCTURAL (example calls its own fn). (3) **Use `grepl(..., fixed=TRUE)`** for the `<fn>(` check — `"\\("` triggers "unrecognized escape" depending on quoting, and `fixed=TRUE` also requires the literal `(` (a call, not a mention). (4) **`document()` can drift unrelated `.Rd`** under a different roxygen2 version — `git status`-check after and restore any file you didn't intend to change; here it cleanly touched only the 3 intended `.Rd`. (5) **NEWS workflow:** edit `NEWS.Rmd` (source), `rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd")` → `NEWS.md`, and `diff` against a pre-render backup to confirm ONLY the new bullet changed (render was stable here). (6) **`lacy1989Ped` `E` is a FOUNDER** (no ancestors) — use `F` (sire=D, dam=E → {A,B,D,E}) for a non-trivial `getAncestors` integration check. (7) CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:216` still say "Version 1.0.8" (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner's standing keep-call). TDD phase declared every response; **3 phase gates** via `AskUserQuestion` (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR); **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out commit on `add-methodology`.
  - **Honest degenerate RED→GREEN (per Learning 80):** the ORIP tab already ships (#47/#49), so there is no production code and the classic red-to-green does not literally apply — declared at the PRE-RED→RED gate. RED = author teeth-bearing assertions + confirm all 4 blocks self-skip cleanly with opt-in OFF (`SSSS`, 0 fail/error). GREEN = run green against the shipped/installed feature, re-deriving the gate outcomes first. REFACTOR = line-length tidy.
  - **Four opt-in blocks** (each `skip_if_not_installed("shinytest2"/"chromote")` + `skip_on_cran()`): (1) **ONPRC → tab accessible** (`assert_active_pane` + the module's unique body text *"Office of Research Infrastructure Programs"*); (2) **ONPRC → content + download** — `#oripReporting-siteInfo` reports `Center=ONPRC`, the Export ORIP Report button is in the pane, and `app$get_download("oripReporting-downloadORIPReport")` yields a `Category/Metric/Value` CSV with a `Center=ONPRC` Site row (deterministic — the handler writes the Site section even with no pedigree loaded); (3) **no config (stock app) → tab ABSENT** (`navigate_to_tab`→FALSE AND `grepl("oripReporting-", body)`→FALSE); (4) **SNPRC config → tab ABSENT** (proves the gate keys on `center`, not mere config presence).
  - **Config-injecting fixture** (the load-bearing design move): a LOCAL `build_config_app_dir(center)` writes a temp `app.R` that `Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with a complete documented-format .nprcgenekeepr_config>)` BEFORE `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` — S84/S85's HOME-override Phase-3E recipe **promoted to a reusable test fixture**. The POSITIVE case can't use the stock `create_test_app()` app (no config → tab hidden); the NO-CONFIG negative case rides the stock app for free. The builder reuses the opt-in gate by calling `create_test_app()` for its **skip side-effect** (path discarded) — **NO change to `helper-shinytest2.R`**. Config dir lives INSIDE the app dir so one `unlink(recursive=TRUE)` (registered AFTER `app$stop()`) cleans both.
  - **GREEN verification:** independently confirmed the three gate outcomes in-process FIRST (`getSiteInfo()` parse → `shouldShowOripTab()`: ONPRC→TRUE, SNPRC→FALSE, no-config→FALSE) — Learning 80b applied to a predicate, so the browser run passed first try; `devtools::install(quick=TRUE)`; then `NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true` single-file → **9/9 expectations PASS** (real Chrome).
  - **REFACTOR (owner-approved):** reflowed 6 over-length lines to ≤80 (split the long `lkPedColumns` config line into the shipped example config's multi-line format, which `getTokenList` parses); re-verified parsing (`center=ONPRC`, 7 `lkPedColumns` tokens, gate TRUE) + a **fresh 9/9 browser run** (the config line is content the app reads) + full clean-regression 0/0 + lint clean.
  - **[news-vs-changelog]:** test-only (no `R/` code, no user-facing feature; the ORIP feature landed in NEWS at S83/S84) → **CHANGELOG only** (S86 entry). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** `git add` only the test file + doc/notes; excluded `.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS.** The browser E2E run **IS** the runtime verification (strongest kind): real Chrome via `chromote`, real reactive graph, real config-driven ONPRC gating, real CSV download. All 4 blocks pass against the **installed** app — 9/9 expectations — including the ONPRC-gated tab present+navigable, `#oripReporting-siteInfo` rendering ONPRC, the report CSV's `Center=ONPRC` row, and the tab genuinely ABSENT under both no-config and SNPRC. `shinytest2`+`chromote` are installed here, so this ran for real (`NOT_CRAN=true` + install-first required — Learning 78).
