**Key files (this session):** **NEW (committed):** `tests/testthat/test-e2e-orip-module.R` (4 opt-in AppDriver blocks / 9 expectations + the local `build_config_app_dir(center)` fixture). **CHANGED (docs):** `CHANGELOG.md` (S86 entry), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (Learning 84 at tail), `SESSION_NOTES.md` (this handoff). **Read firsthand (unchanged):** `tests/testthat/test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R` (the sibling pattern), `R/modORIPReporting.R` (UI body text + `output$siteInfo` + the two `downloadHandler`s `:231`/`:294`), `tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R` (`create_test_app` opt-in gate `:200`, `create_app_driver`, `navigate_to_tab`, `assert_active_pane`, `get_html_safe`, `E2E_TIMEOUT`), `tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R` (the injected-`siteInfo` mount tests), `R/shouldShowOripTab.R` (`isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center=="ONPRC")`), `R/appUI.R:13-18` (the `showOrip` gate), `R/appServer.R:281-292` (gated `modORIPReportingServer` mount), `R/getSiteInfo.R` (parses the file when present; requires all 7 params), `R/getConfigFileName.R:16` (HOME-based), `inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config` (documented multi-line format, center=SNPRC), `inst/shinytest/app.R` (the stock app: `appUI()`/`appServer`, no config). **Transient (NOT committed):** `/tmp/s86_confirm.R`, `/tmp/s86_confirm2.R`.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The ORIP E2E is OPT-IN** (`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true`); it skips cleanly otherwise (4 skips, reason "On CRAN" when `NOT_CRAN` unset). To run it: `NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true` + `devtools::install()` FIRST (it drives the INSTALLED app). (2) **The POSITIVE/SNPRC cases boot a config-injecting `app.R`** (HOME-override) — the stock `create_test_app()` app has NO config so it can only serve the no-config-NEGATIVE block. The builder reuses the opt-in gate via `create_test_app()`'s skip side-effect (no shared-helper change). (3) **The config the fixture writes must have all 7 params** (`getParamDef` `stop()`s on a missing one when a file exists); `center` is the only one the GATE reads, but all 7 must be present to parse. (4) **`getConfigFileName` reads `Sys.getenv("HOME")`** — that's the lever; the generated `app.R` must `Sys.setenv(HOME=...)` BEFORE `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`, and the HOME path is embedded as a `normalizePath(winslash="/")` absolute literal. (5) **`.lintr` excludes `tests/`** — `lintr::lint()` on a test file returns "No lints found" regardless of content; I still kept all lines ≤80 by hand (the sibling's max is 82). (6) The browser E2E run IS the Phase-3E verification (FM #24) — there is no separate smoke needed. (7) CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:216` still say "Version 1.0.8" (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner's standing keep-call). (9) **Default `gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use `gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

### What Session 85 Did
**Deliverable:** Fix **#50** — the modular app's config-loading observer (`R/appServer.R:58-68`) used `read.table(sep="=")`, which crashes `runModularApp()` on boot whenever a config file in the documented format (`inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config`: comments, blank lines, multi-line quoted/comma values) is present. **(DONE — NEW exported `loadSiteConfig()` routes config through the tolerant `getSiteInfo()` parser wrapped in `tryCatch`; observer collapses to a one-liner; new tests 8/8; full suite 0 failed/0 error; lint 0; Phase-3E AppDriver boots the REAL documented config that previously crashed → PASS. #50 CLOSED on owner confirmation 2026-06-16.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-15
**Status:** **DONE.** Development workstream under **Strict TDD**. TDD phase declared every response; **3 phase gates** via `AskUserQuestion` (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR) **+ a separate pre-RED approach question** (single-source-of-truth vs minimal-tryCatch, posed before RED); **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out commit on `add-methodology`.
  - **Pre-RED decision (owner, via `AskUserQuestion`): Single source of truth** (issue's suggested fix #1) over a minimal `tryCatch`-only patch — parse via the tolerant `getSiteInfo()` path so documented-format configs actually LOAD, not merely fail-soft. **De-risked firsthand:** `shared$config` is passed to `modInputServer`/`modPedigreeServer` but referenced by NEITHER module body (`grep -n "config" R/modInput.R R/modPedigree.R` → only `@param`/signature, default `config = NULL`, ignored), so reshaping it from a `read.table` data.frame to the `getSiteInfo()` named list has zero runtime consumer impact.
  - **RED:** NEW `tests/testthat/test_loadSiteConfig.R` (5 tests / 8 expectations): (1) no-file → `NULL`; (2) documented `example_nprcgenekeepr_config` → list, `center=="SNPRC"`, `lkPedColumns>=6` [THE crash case]; (3) malformed config (missing `center`) → `NULL`, no throw [`tryCatch` safety net]; (4) characterization guard — `read.table(sep="=")` errors on the documented file [root-cause lock, green throughout, honestly classified as NOT a RED driver]; (5) structural — `deparse(appServer)` contains `loadSiteConfig`, NOT `read.table`. Confirmed RED: 1/2/3 error (`could not find function "loadSiteConfig"`), 5 fails (observer still on `read.table`), 4 passes. Rest of suite untouched (only a new test file added).
  - **GREEN (2 edits + `document()`):** NEW exported `R/loadSiteConfig.R` = `getConfigFileName(Sys.info())` → `NULL` if no file, else `tryCatch(getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE), error → futile.logger::flog.warn + NULL)`; rewrote the observer to `observe({ shared$config <- loadSiteConfig() })`. `document()` → NAMESPACE export + `man/loadSiteConfig.Rd`. **REFACTOR (owner-chosen small readability tweak):** added a bidirectional `@seealso \link{loadSiteConfig}` to `appServer`'s roxygen, re-`document()`'d; no behavior change, full suite still 0/0.
  - **Verification:** new tests 8/8 green; full clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error** (the 5 warnings are the designed `flog.warn` safety-net logs from test 3); `lintr` **0 lints** on all 3 changed files (the `object_usage` warning for the brand-new fn cleared after `devtools::install()`).
  - **[news-vs-changelog]:** user-facing (a startup-crash bug fix) → **BOTH** `NEWS.Rmd`→`NEWS.md` (new bug-fix bullet under `1.1.0.9000`, rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY that bullet) **AND** `CHANGELOG.md` (S85 entry). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** `git add` only source/test/doc/notes; excluded `.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS.** `AppDriver` boot of the **installed** app with the **REAL documented `example_nprcgenekeepr_config` (center=SNPRC) present** (HOME-override-in-`app.R` recipe; `NOT_CRAN=true`; dev installed first) — the exact file that crashed boot before this fix. App reached a stable state (`AppDriver$new` returned, `mainNavbar`="Home"), navigation to "Genetic Value Analysis" worked (reactive graph alive), **0** `read.table`/"did not have 2 elements" crash lines, **0** non-`shinyBS` error-level logs. **This is the boot S84 had to SIDESTEP with a stripped single-line config; the documented format now boots clean** — the designed payoff of S84's flag-and-file.

**Session 84 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 85): Score 9/10.** S84 **pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1)** with the exact TDD shape: *"Fix #50 … Real TDD: RED = parse `example_nprcgenekeepr_config` through the observer's path and assert no error / correct `shared$config`; GREEN = parse via `getSiteInfo`/`getParamDef` (single source of truth) or `tryCatch`+warn; Phase-3E = boot WITH a documented-format config present (use this session's HOME-override `app.R` recipe)."* Every load-bearing element held firsthand: the **single-source-of-truth direction** (owner-confirmed), the **root cause + file:line** (`appServer.R:63`, `read.table(sep="=")`, from `6457a3a3`), the **HOME-override-in-`app.R` Phase-3E recipe** (reused verbatim — and it booted the documented config clean), the `object_usage_linter` false-positive, the `gh --json` gotcha, the 2-untracked-files disposition, the stale version string, the branch model, and the baselines (dashboard 98/100, 18 issues, ghost-check clean). **Zero time lost on what to build, the root cause, or the runtime recipe.** **The −1:** S84 didn't flag that `shared$config` is passed-but-UNUSED by both modules — the fact that determined the reshape was safe (I found it by grep); and "parse through the observer's path" slightly under-specifies that an `observe()` isn't unit-testable in isolation, so the real move is to extract a pure helper (`loadSiteConfig`) for the test seam. Both genuinely the implementer's to discover. ROI: very high.

  - **Two pre-RED decisions (owner, via one `AskUserQuestion`):** (a) **product fork → Hide unless a real ONPRC config** — show only when an actual config file exists AND `center=="ONPRC"`; the `getSiteInfo()` default fallback (`center="ONPRC"` with no file, true in dev/CI) hides the tab. (b) **approach → Build-time conditional `tabPanel`** (not dynamic `insertTab`) — `appUI()` is evaluated ONCE at `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` construction and the deployment center is fixed per server, so the tab's presence is a per-deployment CONSTANT, not a per-session reactive (dynamic `insertTab` is for DATA-driven tabs like Error List).
  - **RED:** NEW `tests/testthat/test_shouldShowOripTab.R` (5 pure-predicate cases) + rewrote `tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R` to INJECT a `siteInfo` list — tab PRESENT under ONPRC+existing-file, ABSENT under SNPRC / non-existent-file — plus a `deparse(appServer)` assertion that the server mount is gated on `shouldShowOripTab`. All failed for the right reason (undefined function / unused `siteInfo` arg / un-gated mount); rest of suite unaffected (only test files changed).
  - **GREEN (3 edits):** NEW exported `R/shouldShowOripTab.R` = `isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center == "ONPRC")` (mirrors `shouldShowChangedColsTab`; `isTRUE(==)` not `identical` → names/NA/NULL/length-safe); parameterized `appUI(siteInfo = getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE))` with `if (isTRUE(showOrip)) tabPanel("ORIP Reporting", …)`; gated the `modORIPReportingServer` mount in `appServer.R` on the same predicate. `document()` → NAMESPACE export + `man/shouldShowOripTab.Rd` + `man/appUI.Rd`. **REFACTOR = confirmed no-op** (kept the sibling inline-call idiom; no helper extraction — would break the appServer structural test's intent).
  - **Verification:** new tests 5/5 + 4/4 (9 expectations); full clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error**; `lintr` **0 lints** on all 5 changed files (the `object_usage` warning for the brand-new fn cleared after `devtools::install()`, like its installed siblings).
  - **[news-vs-changelog]:** user-facing (visible-tab behavior change) → **BOTH** `NEWS.Rmd`→`NEWS.md` (ORIP bullet augmented with the ONPRC-gating note; rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY that bullet) **AND** `CHANGELOG.md` (S84 entry). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** `git add` only source/test/doc/notes; excluded `.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS (3/3 config scenarios).** `AppDriver` boots of the **installed** app, each via a generated `app.R` that `Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>)` BEFORE `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` (controls the real `getSiteInfo`→config path in-process): **ONPRC config** → ORIP tab present + navigable (active pane = ORIP), all 5 module outputs register values, 4 siblings intact; **SNPRC config** → tab ABSENT (UI + server), siblings intact; **no config** → tab ABSENT, siblings intact. 0 ORIP-namespaced ERROR-level logs (the 5 `oripReporting` lines are INFO `shiny:value` registrations; the only errors are the pre-existing app-wide `shinyBS` noise). **First app boot ever done WITH a config file present → surfaced pre-existing bug #50 (below).**

**Discovered (out of scope) → NEW #50:** the pre-existing config-loading observer (`appServer.R:63`, from `6457a3a3`, 2025-12-29) uses `read.table(configFile, header=TRUE, sep="=")`, which **cannot parse the documented config format** (`example_nprcgenekeepr_config`: comments, blank lines, multi-line quoted values) and **crashes the modular app on boot** whenever such a file is present — latent in dev/CI only because no config file exists there. **NOT fixed** (unrelated to #49); sidestepped for the smoke with a stripped single-line `key=value` config both `read.table` AND `getParamDef` accept; filed **#50** with root cause + repro + suggested fix.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The ORIP tab gate is BUILD-TIME** — decided when `appUI()`/`appServer` first run (per-deployment constant). Correct because the config is per-server, but it does NOT react to a mid-session config change. (2) **`appUI()` now takes a `siteInfo` param** (defaulted to `getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE)`) — tests inject it; production callers (`runModularApp`, `inst/shinytest/app.R`) call `appUI()` with no args (unchanged behavior). (3) **Pre-existing bug #50:** the `appServer` config observer's `read.table(sep="=")` crashes the app on boot with a DOCUMENTED-format config file. For ANY app-boot-with-config test, use a stripped single-line `key=value` config (all 7 params: center, baseUrl, schemaName, folderPath, queryName, lkPedColumns, mapPedColumns — multi-values SPACE-separated, no quotes/commas/comments) that both `read.table` and `getParamDef` accept. (4) **Phase-3E HOME-override recipe:** generate a temp `app.R` doing `Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>)` BEFORE `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`, then `AppDriver$new(<that dir>)` (`NOT_CRAN=true` + install dev first). One AppDriver per config scenario. (5) **Classify AppDriver logs by LEVEL/text, not namespace mention** — `shiny:value <ns>-<output>` lines are INFO registrations (success), not errors; genuine errors are `error`-level minus the pre-existing app-wide `shinyBS is not defined` noise. (6) **`object_usage_linter` false-positives a brand-new package function** ("no visible global function") until `devtools::install()`; re-lint after install to confirm clean. (7) CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:216` still say "Version 1.0.8" (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner's standing keep-call). (9) **Default `gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use `gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

**Gotchas:** (1) **The new E2E test is OPT-IN** (`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true`) and **drives the INSTALLED package** — to run it: `devtools::install()` dev code first, then `NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true Rscript -e '...test_file(...)'`. Without `NOT_CRAN=true` AppDriver aborts "On CRAN"; without install the installed copy may be stale (Learning 78). (2) **`shinytest2` + `chromote` ARE installed here** — the browser E2E genuinely runs; don't assume "no Chrome → skip". (3) **`PP_EXPECTED_CANDIDATES <- 50L` is a regression lock** — if the fixture or `getPotentialParents` ever changes the count, this fails by design; re-derive via the pipeline (`read.csv` → `runQcStudbook(...)$cleaned` → `setPopulation` → `getPotentialParents(_, 2.0, 210L)`) and update. Both `processedPedigree` and `pedigreeData` yield 50 today. (4) **The table assertion uses DT's *"of 50 entries"* info text** (mirrors the pedigree e2e's *"of 375 entries"*); a DT phrasing change would require updating it. (5) **`shared$currentPedigree` = the FILTERED `pedigreeData()`** (`modPedigree.R:344`), auto-populated by the observer at `appServer.R:252-255` once the studbook is ready — but blocks 3/4 still visit Pedigree Browser first to faithfully mirror S81's manual flow and dodge any output-suspension flakiness. (6) **`ExamplePedigree.csv` is the right no-`fromCenter` graceful-degradation input** (QC-clean, no colony-origin field); the populated path needs `rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv`. (7) CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:198` still say "Version 1.0.8" (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner's standing keep-call). (9) **Default `gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use `gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

The owner ran it live and **caught a real defect in my staged file** that my headless pre-check had blessed → I fixed and re-verified against the app's EXACT ingest path before close.
  - **NEW `inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv`** (375 animals = `nprcgenekeepr::rhesusPedigree` + `fromCenter = TRUE`; unknown parents written as **literal `NA`** matching `ExamplePedigree.csv`). Verified through the real pipeline (app reader → `runQcStudbook` → `setPopulation` → `getPotentialParents`): QC clean, `fromCenter` preserved, **50 animals with candidates**. Owner chose the `rhesusPedigree` source (vs full-ExamplePedigree / modify-in-place) via `AskUserQuestion`.
  - **Defect caught by the click-through (FM #24 — replication diverged from the app):** my first file wrote unknowns as **empty cells** (`write.csv(na="")`); the app's text reader (`modInput.R:274` = `read.table(..., fill=TRUE, quote="\"")` with **default `na.strings="NA"`**) reads `""` as the empty string, so `""` landed in **both** the sire and dam columns → `correctParentSex.R:71` `intersect(sires,dams)` flagged *"Subject(s) listed as both sire and dam"* (the empty offending id = the "cell empty" the owner saw). My pre-check had used `na.strings=c("","NA")`, silently converting `""`→NA and masking it. Rewrote with `na="NA"` and re-verified with the app's exact reader → clean.
  - **Diagnosed two non-defects firsthand from the code (not guesses):** (1) the *"Column name case changed: fromCenter → fromcenter"* warning is **cosmetic** — `fixColumnNames.R:20` lowercases all headers (logging the caseChange), `:61` restores `fromcenter`→`fromCenter`; the cleaned studbook keeps `fromCenter`, which is exactly what `getPotentialParents` (case-sensitive `names(ped)=="fromCenter"`) needs. Same noise hits `recordStatus`/`geographicOrigin`. (2) *"No data available in table"* = the empty **Errors** tab DT placeholder (no errors). The earlier repeat of *"both sire and dam"* was a **stale upload** — cleared once the owner re-read the rewritten file.
  - **[news-vs-changelog]** → **CHANGELOG only** (verification + a test-support example dataset + issue close = dev-process history; the *feature* already landed in `NEWS.md` at S80; no `R/` code changed). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** `git add` only `inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` + `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` + `CHANGELOG.md`; excluded `.DS_Store` and the 2 standing untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

Existing tabs intact.

### What Session 74 Did
**Deliverable:** Implement **#31** — replace the dam-exclusion "hack" (fixed ±½-year birth window) in `getPotentialParents` with a principled gestation-derived window driven by the **existing** `maxGestationalPeriod` parameter (owner-decided S73: reuse/extend that param, NO new parameter). The near-term, low-lift sub-task of umbrella **#45**. **(DONE — feature shipped; `devtools::check()` 0/0/0; full suite 0/0; #31 left OPEN, awaiting owner confirm to close.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-13
**Status:** **DONE.** Code session under strict **TDD: RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped)** — a separate pre-RED scope/approach decision + all three transition gates (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR-skipped) posed via `AskUserQuestion`; TDD phase declared atop **every** response. **0 stakeholder corrections.** One close-out commit on `add-methodology`.
  - **The change (ONE rule, one line of logic):** a female who delivered another offspring within `maxGestationalPeriod` days of the focal birth cannot have gestated the focal animal (a female bears one offspring at a time), so she is excluded as a candidate dam. The `births` window (`getPotentialParents.R:83-85`) now uses `pUnknown$birth[i] ± maxGestationalPeriod` instead of `± (dYear / 2L)` (182.5 d). The `:106` dam exclusion (`potentialDams[!id %in% births$dam, ]`) and `:92-95` comment block had the TODO "hack" replaced with a principled explanation; the `@param maxGestationalPeriod` roxygen (now `~:17-27`) documents it as the conservative MAX gestation (e.g. 210 for rhesus vs ~165 typical), its dual sire-retention + dam-exclusion use, and the **intentional** sire(conception)/dam(birth) exit-check asymmetry.
  - **Owner decision (pre-RED, via `AskUserQuestion`):** **minimal principled change** — only the `births` window; document the exit-check asymmetry as correct (NOT changed); leave the preferential band untouched (proven a no-op); recompute fixtures with per-delta justification; keep `maxGestationalPeriod=210L` in tests. Applied exactly as chosen.
  - **Recompute-don't-inherit (Learning 70/72 applied to a code change → Learning 73):** in PRE-RED I read issue #31/#45 + `getPotentialParents.R` + `test_getPotentialParents.R` firsthand, then built a **throwaway read-only oracle** (`/tmp/s74_explore.R`, never touching `R/`) to quantify the behavior change BEFORE writing tests — every fixture delta derived independently + justified biologically, not from running the production change.
  - **TDD trail (RED→GREEN):** RED edited ONLY `test_getPotentialParents.R` — recomputed exact-set fixtures (`dams_1` drops `0B7XRI`/`PHCADH`; `dams_4` drops `1SIP4V`/`DMI0QY`/`HV7LZ3`, each with a justification comment citing the conflicting-offspring offset) + added 2 driving tests (a hand-verifiable **synthetic-exclusion** pedigree + a **differential-responsiveness** test, acceptance criterion #2). **Confirmed RED: 4 expected failures** for the right reasons; the 4 unaffected tests passed. ### What Session 71 Did
**Deliverable:** Implement GitHub issue **#44** (full umbrella scope) and its **#38** sub-task — configurable auto-generated unknown-ID format (default `"U%04d"`, **byte-identical back-compat with no config**) from a single source of truth: exported `getAutoIdFormat()`/`setAutoIdFormat()` + an internal `isGeneratedUnknownId()` predicate replacing all 7 detection literals across 4 files + 2 generators; case-handling reconciled to case-sensitive. **(DONE — feature shipped; #44 + #38 implemented + `devtools::check()`-green, awaiting owner confirm to close.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-13
**Status:** **DONE.** Code session under strict **TDD: RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped)** — all three transition gates posed via `AskUserQuestion`, plus a separate pre-RED scope/approach decision (3 questions). TDD phase declared atop **every** response. **0 stakeholder corrections.** One close-out commit on `add-methodology`.
  - **Owner decisions (pre-RED, via `AskUserQuestion`, 3 questions):** **(Scope)** full #44; **(Detection)** prefix-only, byte-identical (`startsWith(id, <literal prefix before first %>)` — preserves today's exact matching, incl. the existing over-match of real prefix-IDs); **(Case rule)** case-sensitive (match generation's uppercase prefix; reconciles `reportGV`+`obfuscateId`, which were insensitive). All applied exactly as chosen.
  - **Recompute-don't-inherit (Learning 70 applied to a feature):** re-read the 8 load-bearing files firsthand to verify S70's subsystem map against the working tree BEFORE declaring RED — confirmed the 2 generators + 7 detection sites + the case-divergence, rather than trusting the handoff.
  - **Evidence-based decision aid (→ Learning 71b):** grepped the test corpus and found the lowercase `"u001"` in `test_obfuscateId.R:28` — the exact datum that makes "reconcile case-sensitivity" a concrete, test-breaking fork. The case-rule `AskUserQuestion` cited that specific test, so the owner chose against evidence.
  - **New code:** `R/autoIdFormat.R` — `getAutoIdFormat()` = `getOption("nprcgenekeepr.autoIdFormat", "U%04d")` + `setAutoIdFormat(format)` (validates: single non-NA char, non-empty literal prefix, sprintf-template-consuming-one-integer; returns previous invisibly) [both **exported**]; internal `getAutoIdPrefix()` + `isGeneratedUnknownId()` (case-sensitive, **NA-preserving like the `startsWith`/`stri_sub` it replaces** → a true drop-in) [`@noRd`].
  - **Threaded through (all 7 literals + 2 generators centralized):** `addUIds(ped, format = getAutoIdFormat())` mints via `sprintf(format, …)` (≡ default); `obfuscateId` mints with `getAutoIdPrefix()` + `size - nchar(prefix)` and guards via the predicate; `removeAutoGenIds` (×3), `modPedigree.R:290` display filter, `reportGV.R:137-140` founder counts all route detection through the single predicate. `removeAutoGenIds`'s "use a function call" TODO resolved (docstring updated). `qcStudbook` **unchanged** (its `addUIds(sb)` picks up the option via the default — smallest blast radius). `modPedigree.R:112` tooltip notes configurability.
  - **TDD trail (RED→GREEN):** RED wrote 2 NEW test files (`test_autoIdFormat.R`, `test_removeAutoGenIds.R`) + edited `test_obfuscateId.R:27-32` to the case-sensitive contract, and **confirmed red** (new fns undefined → error; obfuscate test fails vs current insensitive code). GREEN: the new file + 5 edits + `devtools::document()`. **Only ONE existing test changed** (`test_obfuscateId`); `test_addUIds`/`test_qcStudbook`/`test_modPedigree` pass UNCHANGED = the byte-identical back-compat proof (→ Learning 71a).
  - **Verification:** **Full suite 0 failed / 0 error / 159 skip** (985 results; the 5 warnings are pre-existing `test_modPyramid.R` baseline). **`devtools::check()` exit 0, 1 NOTE** — the `spelling.R` comparison vs a stale `spelling.Rout.save`; attributed to **pre-existing baseline** (`spell_check_package` + check word list flag **0** of my identifiers among 54 words → not introduced/worsened; left as housekeeping, → Learning 71d). Lint clean under `load_all` (the `options()` setter carries `# nolint: undesirable_function_linter` per the `getPyramidPlot.R` precedent — a public permanent setter). Exhaustive `grep` confirmed **no remaining auto-ID `"U"` literal** in `R/` (only the unrelated sex-factor level M/F/U/H). **[news-vs-changelog] → BOTH** (user-facing feature): `NEWS.md` NEW-48 + `CHANGELOG.md` S71 under `[Unreleased]`.

**⟢ Carried — Session 68 (implement #35 descendants, DONE; full detail below):**

### What Session 68 Did
**Deliverable:** Implement GitHub issue #35 — include **descendants** in the Pedigree Browser's "Trim pedigree" filtering (ancestor-inclusion was already done per S67). Strict TDD, **Option A (strict lineal)** per owner. **(DONE — feature shipped; #35 implemented, awaiting owner confirm to close.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-13
**Status:** **DONE.** First real **code** session after the S57–S67 non-code run → genuine **RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped)** with all three phase gates posed via `AskUserQuestion`, plus a separate pre-RED **Option A/B approach decision**. **0 stakeholder corrections.** One close-out commit pending on `add-methodology`.
  - **The approach (owner-chosen via `AskUserQuestion`, pre-RED): Option A — strict lineal.** Trimmed pedigree = focal ∪ transitive ancestors ∪ transitive descendants; **no collaterals** (siblings/cousins/mates). (Option B = reuse `getPedDirectRelatives`, which adds collaterals = whole connected pedigree — declined.)
  - **New code:** `R/getDescendantPedigree.R` — new **exported** transitive-offspring closure (`repeat { getOffspring → break when nothing new }`), the downward mirror of `getProbandPedigree`. `R/modPedigree.R:292-307` — trim block now unions the ancestor closure (`trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative=FALSE, addBackParents=FALSE)`) with the new descendant closure: `ped <- ped[ped$id %in% union(ancestors$id, descendants$id), ]`. **UI help text** (`:124-126`) changed from the "include only **relatives**" over-promise S67 flagged → "the **ancestors and descendants** of the focal animals"; **docstring** (`:166-167`) updated to match.
  - **TDD trail (RED then GREEN):** RED wrote 9 failing assertions and CONFIRMED red — `test_getDescendantPedigree.R` errored (`could not find function`) + the 2 new integration tests failed (`"GC" %in% ped$id` false under ancestors-only). GREEN: created the helper + union edit + docstring/help-text + `devtools::document()` (man page + NAMESPACE export) + `NEWS.md` NEW-47.
  - **The regression catch (→ Learning 68):** the full-suite clean-regression read surfaced a SECOND stale test in a *different* file — `test_modPedigree.R:419` ("trims pedigree based on focal animals") asserted the OLD ancestors-only result (`nrow==3`, `expect_false("D")`) for focal `{A,C}`; D is a child of focal A → now a correctly-included descendant. Updated it to the new contract (`nrow==4`, `{A,B,C,D}` in, half-sib E still out) with reasoning. I'd only updated the obvious `_processing` file in RED; the regression read (CLAUDE.md Build/Test/Verify) is the backstop that caught the one RED missed.
  - **Verification:** **Full suite 972 tests / 0 failed / 0 error / 159 skip.** Lint clean — the lone `object_usage_linter` warning ("no visible global function definition for 'getDescendantPedigree'") proven an **install-staleness artifact**: 0 warnings once linted with `pkgload::load_all()` (resolves against installed namespace; the new fn is dev-only until reinstall). [news-vs-changelog] → BOTH (user-facing feature): `NEWS.md` NEW-47 + `CHANGELOG.md` S68 under `[Unreleased]`. BACKLOG.md has no #35/descendant ref (grep-verified — nothing to prune).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **DONE via `shiny::testServer`.** The deliverable changes the `modPedigreeServer` `pedigreeData` reactive — the 2 integration tests in `test_modPedigree_processing.R` (+ the updated `test_modPedigree.R` trim test) drive that exact reactive through the real server pipeline (`processedPedigree` → trim union) and confirm the new behavior is active (descendant `GC` included, collaterals `C`/`E` excluded). That is the deterministic runtime verification for a Shiny-module change; no separate interactive `runModularApp()` browser launch was needed (testServer exercises the changed code path directly).

(2) **`getDescendantPedigree` is the new descendants-only primitive** — `getProbandPedigree`=ancestors(up), `getDescendantPedigree`=descendants(down), `getPedDirectRelatives`=both+collaterals, `getOffspring`/`getParents`=single-level. (3) **The trim is strict-lineal by design** — a displayed descendant's *other parent* (mate) is intentionally NOT pulled in, so the trimmed table can show an animal whose `sire`/`dam` isn't a row; that is the approved Option-A semantics, not a bug. (4) **`object_usage_linter` will warn on `getDescendantPedigree` until the package is reinstalled** — it's an install-staleness artifact (0 warnings under `load_all`); don't "fix" it. **⟢ Carried — Session 67 (re-scope #35 to descendants, DONE; full detail below):**
**Task (Session 67 — 2026-06-13):** Execute S66 SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 — **re-scope GitHub issue #35** ("Include ancestors and descendants in pedigree filtering") to focus on the remaining **descendants** work, after firsthand-verifying that ancestor-inclusion is implemented and descendant-inclusion is not. **(DONE — #35 re-scoped + retitled, kept OPEN.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-13
**Status:** **DONE.** Issue-grooming / re-scope session (no production code or tests written; **TDD phase = N/A** declared every response — same non-code classification S57/S61–S66 used). One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. **No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md` changes.**
  - **The finding (firsthand — confirmed the handoff directionally, added the depth it couldn't):** #35's compound "ancestors AND descendants" ask is **half done**. **Ancestors DONE** — `R/modPedigree.R:292-302`: when the "Trim pedigree based on focal animals" checkbox is on, it calls `trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative = FALSE, addBackParents = FALSE)` → `getProbandPedigree()` (`R/getProbandPedigree.R:24-40`), an **upward** `sire`/`dam` closure (ancestors only). The module docstring already says so (`R/modPedigree.R:166`: "Optionally trimming to **ancestors**…"). **Descendants NOT** — neither `trimPedigree` nor `getProbandPedigree` walks downward; `getOffspring()` is single-level only.
  - **Two stale references the inherited framing did NOT flag (caught firsthand):** (1) the issue's cited "Current Code" (lines **246-253**, a `# TODO: Include ancestors and descendants` placeholder) **no longer exists** — replaced by the live ancestor logic at 292-302; (2) its "Suggested Implementation" called `trimPedigree(focalIds(), ped, ancestors = TRUE, descendants = TRUE)` — **that signature does not exist** (`trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative, addBackParents)`). Copying the suggested call forward would have handed the implementer a non-compiling spec (→ Learning 67(a)).
  - **The semantic fork surfaced (not silently picked):** "include descendants" has two non-equivalent implementations — **(A) strict lineal**: add a downward closure mirroring `getProbandPedigree`'s loop (repeated `getOffspring()` to closure), union with the ancestor set = focal's ancestors+descendants only; **(B) reuse `getPedDirectRelatives()`** (`R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:46-59`), which already loops `getParents()` AND `getOffspring()` to closure but thereby also pulls in **collateral** relatives (sibs/cousins/mates) = the whole connected pedigree, broader than lineal. The package has the both-directions primitive but **no transitive descendants-only helper**.
  - **Applied (owner-chosen via `AskUserQuestion`, 2 questions):** **FORM = rewrite the body** + retitle to "Include descendants in pedigree filtering (ancestors already implemented)" + dated verification comment; **SCOPE = document BOTH options** as an open design choice (left for the implementation session). Did `gh issue edit 35 --title … --body-file` + `gh issue comment 35` (comment `#issuecomment-4699260833`). **Issue kept OPEN.**
  - **Verified the edit landed:** title updated + `state: open` re-read via `gh api`; 5 grep checks on the saved body all PASS (cites live 292-302; notes the non-existent `ancestors=,descendants=` API; both Option A + Option B present; references `getPedDirectRelatives`; notes the stale 246-253 placeholder is gone). Also noted the UI help text already over-promises ("…include only **relatives** of the focal animals", `R/modPedigree.R:125`) while behavior is ancestors-only — flagged in the body as a label-alignment item.
  - **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only (S67 entry under `[Unreleased]`; dev-process/grooming history, not a user-facing release note → NOT `NEWS.md`; **BACKLOG.md has no #35 / descendants reference** — verified by grep, nothing to prune). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** explicit `git add` of only `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked: `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

  - **The finding — far staler than the handoff anticipated:** S64 (inheriting the S62 audit) framed #37 as "strike the resolved Shiny-module + genotype rows; keep the still-accurate inventory" (≈7 functions). A from-scratch call-graph reachability recomputation found **45 of the 70 listed functions are now used by the app** — the inventory was ~64% stale, not 2 clusters. **Verified totals (current code): 155 exports / 116 used / 39 unused** (the issue claimed 108 / 38 / 70).
  - **Method (firsthand, reproducible):** `pkgload::load_all`, seed the call graph at `{runModularApp, runGeneKeepR, appUI, appServer}`, take the transitive closure of `codetools::findGlobals(f)$functions ∩ package-functions`; exported names outside the closure = unused. Produced concrete call paths for the flipped set (e.g. `rankSubjects`: `appServer → modGeneticValueServer → reportGV → orderReport → rankSubjects`; the 4 Shiny modules called directly by `appServer`; the genotype trio `getGenotypes`/`addGenotype`/`hasGenotype` via `modInputServer`; `filterKinMatrix/Report/Threshold` via the breeding-group + GV modules). Confirmed all 22 "still unused" are genuinely unreachable.
  - **Adversarial check of the static method's blind spots:** findGlobals misses dynamic + S3 dispatch, so I checked both explicitly: the ONLY string-dispatched call in `R/` is `do.call("rbind", …)` (base), and **none of the 22 still-unused names appear as a string literal anywhere in `R/`** → no dynamic invocation the closure missed; the 3 S3 methods (`print.summary.*`, `summary.nprcgenekeeprErr`) aren't dispatched on the app path (the app's `summary()` calls hit `summary.default` on numeric vectors). Classification held: **45 flipped / 22 unused / 3 S3 = 70.**

Posted a timeline pointer comment (`#issuecomment-4696756359`) so the silent body edit is visible to watchers. **Issue kept OPEN** — the 39 genuinely-unused exports remain the actionable surface.

**⟢ Carried — Session 64 (verify + close #8, DONE; full detail below):**
**Task (Session 64 — 2026-06-12):** Execute S63 SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 — verify issue #8 ("Improve handling of non-founder animals without either parents assigned") firsthand against current code, then **CLOSE #8 with a caveat note**. **(DONE — #8 CLOSED, strengthened caveat.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-12
**Status:** **DONE.** Verify-and-close session (no production code or tests written; **TDD phase = N/A** declared every response — same classification S57/S61/S63 used closing #30/#34/#14). One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. **No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md` changes.**
  - **The finding — re-graded from the audit's footnote-caveat to a STRENGTHENED caveat (with a reproduction):** #8's two proposed solutions are genuinely implemented, BUT firsthand verification + a direct reproduction showed the fix is gated on the optional `origin` column and **silently recreates the original bug in the default (no-`origin`) case** — plus the issue's 2021 simulated-kinship thread is orphaned (the audit never mentioned it). The owner picked the **strengthened-caveat close** via `AskUserQuestion`.
  - **What's implemented (verified firsthand):** **Solution 1 (origin/"From Center" segregation)** — ONPRC-born founders with no offspring are split into a `noParentage` bucket: `R/orderReport.R:31,44-54` (`is.na(rpt$origin) & totalOffspring==0L & id %in% founders`), placed last. **Solution 2 (don't rank them)** — `R/rankSubjects.R:38` sets `value="Undetermined"`, `:44` sets `rank=NA`. **Live** — `reportGV()` → `orderReport(finalData, ped)` (`R/reportGV.R:146`); `finalData` carries `origin` only when the source ped does (`R/reportGV.R:119` `intersect(getIncludeColumns(), names(ped))`). **Green** — `pkgload::load_all` runs: `test_orderReport.R` **8/0/0/0**, `test_rankSubjects.R` **5/0/0/0**.
  - **The caveats (firsthand, beyond the audit's one-liner):** **(1) origin-gating silently no-ops — REPRODUCED.** Whole `noParentage` block wrapped in `if ("origin" %in% names(rpt))` (`R/orderReport.R:31`); `origin` is documented optional (`R/getPossibleCols.R:29`, `R/qcStudbook.R:34`), absent from most bundled datasets. I built a synthetic ped+rpt (a no-parent founder, 0 offspring, high gu) and ran `orderReport()`: *without* `origin` → `value="High Value", rank=1` (the exact #8 bug, no warning); *with* `origin` (NA for the ONPRC animal) → `"Undetermined"/NA`. **(2) No regression pin** — `test_orderReport.R:15-26` runs the origin path but asserts only positional unknown-ID counts; `test_rankSubjects.R` never exercises `noParentage`; the only "Undetermined" in tests is a comment in a usually-skipped e2e (`test-e2e-genetic-value-tutorial.R:109`). **(3) Orphaned thread B** — the 2021 simulated-kinship subsystem (`getPotentialParents`, `createSimKinships`, `kinshipMatricesToKValues`, `summarizeKinshipValues`, `countKinshipValues`, `addKinshipValueCount`, `cumulateSimKinships`) is exported but has NO live caller in `R/` (only tests + `inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R` + `vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd`); the issue's "discuss with Matt … display/use simulated kinship coefficients" design item is unresolved. All three documented in the close comment as candidate future enhancements; **none filed** (new-issue creation outside the approved "close with caveat" scope — S63 lesson; I did not even attempt it this time).
  - **Adversarial close gate (3-lens refute workflow `wf_f37f1b72-b6a`, `Explore` skeptics vs the working tree):** **SPLIT — 1 hold-open + 2 close-with-caveat — reconciled by REPRODUCTION, not vote.** (i) headline-implemented → `refuted=true`, hold-open (high): the origin-gating makes the fix illusory in the common case; claimed to have reproduced a no-parent founder ranked "High Value". I reconstructed it myself (the table above) → **upheld**, which is what upgraded the close from clean→strengthened-caveat. (ii) test-authenticity → `refuted=false`: no assertion pins the branch → **upheld**, caveat not blocker. (iii) intent-completeness → `refuted=false`: simulated-kinship subsystem orphaned (also surfaced the vignette + example file) → **upheld**, severable. The lone dissenter was the decisive one — verifying it firsthand beat tallying 2-of-3.
  - **Closed:** `gh issue comment 8 --body-file` + `gh issue close 8` with the strengthened-caveat resolution comment (both solutions implemented + the reproduced origin-gating failure + the missing test + the orphaned thread B + why-close-anyway). Verified `state=closed`, `closed_at=2026-06-12T22:10:27Z`. **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only (S64 entry under `[Unreleased]`; dev-process/close history, not a user-facing release note → NOT `NEWS.md`; BACKLOG.md has no #8 reference — open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** explicit `git add` of only `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked: `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **N/A and stated** — no runtime behavior changed (closed a GitHub issue + edited build-ignored docs; no `R/` code). The runtime behavior #8 concerns IS verified, though: the firsthand reproduction drove `orderReport()` end-to-end and showed both branches (High Value rank-1 without `origin`; Undetermined/NA with it) — a stronger, cheaper gate than a manual app boot.

  - **What's implemented (verified firsthand):** **Provide** — the modular app's separate-genotype-file mode reads via `getGenotypes`, validates via `checkGenotypeFile` (degrade-to-NULL), integer-codes + merges via `addGenotype` (`R/modInput.R:384-396`); same functions exported for scripting. **Track** — integer `first`/`second` ride the cleaned studbook into `reportGV()` → `getGVGenotype` (`R/reportGV.R:78`) → `geneDrop(genotype=…)` (`:92`) → `calcGU`/`calcFG`. **Live** — `runModularApp()` → `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` → `modInputUI/Server("dataInput")` (`appUI.R:123`, `appServer.R:104`); module exposes `cleanedStudbook`/`genotypeData` reactives. **Pinned** — I ran the genotype tests via `pkgload::load_all`: **278 assertions, 0 fail / 0 err / 0 skip**, incl. the end-to-end `modInputServer` separate-file test (`test_modInput_qcStudbook.R:536-545` asserts `hasGenotype(cleaned)`, integer `first`/`second`, populated `genotypeData()`). The **0 skips** are load-bearing (the `testServer` tests `skip_if_not_installed("shiny")`; shiny is installed → they actually drove the module).
  - **The caveat (deliberate, owner-authored):** `commonPedGenoFile` (combined ped+geno file) UI mode does **not** integer-code string alleles → those genotypes never satisfy `hasGenotype()` / reach gene-drop. This is **at parity with the legacy monolith** and explicitly intentional per the owner's own commit `c9019d51`: *"neither app integer-codes string alleles for a combined file, so common-mode genotypes never reach gene-drop in either."* Documented in the close comment; combined-file tracking noted there as a candidate future enhancement (NOT filed — new-issue creation was outside the approved "close with caveat" scope; the auto-mode classifier correctly denied my attempt to `gh issue create`).
  - **Adversarial close gate (3-lens refute workflow `wf_9d00a37a-6d0`):** **1 confirmed, 2 refuted — and I reconciled EACH verdict firsthand.** (i) live-path → `refuted=false` (matched my read). (ii) intent-completeness → `refuted=true`, REAL → this surfaced the combined-file caveat → re-graded the close. (iii) test-authenticity → `refuted=true`, but an **ARTIFACT**: the agent decompiled the **stale installed binary** (missing the merge block) instead of the source; my `load_all` run (90/0/0/0) + my read of `modInput.R:384-396` proved the source is correct and green. Agents inspect what's installed; the parent runs `load_all` against the working tree.
  - **Closed:** `gh issue close 14` with a resolution comment (implemented-ability evidence + the parity caveat + the firsthand/adversarial verification). Verified `state=closed`, `closed_at=2026-06-12T21:48:24Z`. **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only (S63 entry under `[Unreleased]`; dev-process/close history, not a user-facing release note → NOT `NEWS.md`; BACKLOG.md has no #14 reference — open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** explicit `git add` of only `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked: `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

The runtime behavior #14 concerns IS verified, though: the `shiny::testServer` tests actually drove `modInputServer` end-to-end with the genotype-merge path (0 skips) — a stronger, cheaper gate than a manual app boot.

Fixed with `unsetopt nomatch`. Avoidable given the explicit handoff warning → honest 8 (clean, authoritatively-verified deliverable, 0 corrections, but one round-trip the handoff had pre-warned). No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md` changes.

**Gotchas:** (1) **`.Rbuildignore` is a REGEX file** — every line incl. `#` comments is a perl regex; an unbalanced paren ABORTS `R CMD build`. Keep comments balanced/paren-free; re-build after ANY edit (carried, S59). (2) **zsh `nomatch` ABORTS on an unmatched glob** — `rm -f <glob>` with no match kills the whole script (even with `-f`); prefix `unsetopt nomatch 2>/dev/null; setopt null_glob 2>/dev/null` before any cleanup glob. (3) **Dated cran-comments are now covered by one `^[0-9]+_cran-comments\.md$`** — do NOT re-add per-date exact lines; a new dated file is auto-ignored. NOTE: this tight pattern does NOT dupe-guard (`20250504_cran-comments 2.md` would NOT be ignored) — intentional (cran-comments aren't macOS-synced). (4) **Tight vs broad patterns:** `^methodology_dashboard\.py$`/`^dashboard\.html$`/`^nprcgenekeepr_notes\.txt$` are exact (no dupe-guard); the `<NAME>.*` ones (`RECOMMENDED_SKILLS`, `PED_GV_AUDIT`, `TECH_DEBT_AUDIT`) ARE dupe-guarded — matched to whether the file is sync-prone. (5) **`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` remains untracked on disk** (now build-ignored) — owner's standing keep/remove call; `..Rcheck/00check.log` is build-ignored check residue. (6) DESCRIPTION is `1.1.0.9000` (CLAUDE.md's "1.0.8" is stale) — glob `nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz`. (7) Verify any `.Rbuildignore` change at the BUILD level (stage dummy → `R CMD build` → `tar tzf`), NOT via `devtools::check()` and NOT by eye. **Branch model unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-12
**Status:** **DONE.** Build-config-only change, behavior-none; **TDD phase = N/A** declared every response (no production R code; `.Rbuildignore` is dropped from the built tarball, so there is no shippable `testthat` assertion — RED→GREEN→REFACTOR does not apply, same as S58).   - **The change (`.Rbuildignore:78-85`):** broadened 7 patterns `^NAME\.md$` → `^NAME.*\.md$` for `PROJECT_LEARNINGS`, `CLAUDE`, `SESSION_RUNNER`, `SAFEGUARDS`, `BACKLOG`, `ROADMAP`, `CHANGELOG`.   - **In-flight finding (caught by the build step, fixed, NOT shipped):** **every line in `.Rbuildignore` is a perl regex — including `#` comments** (they only "work" because each is a valid regex matching no real path). My first multi-line comment held an unbalanced `(` → `R CMD build` aborted with `invalid regular expression`. Fixed to balanced/paren-free comment lines + an inline NOTE warning future editors. (Learning 59b.)
  - **Verification (firsthand, both levels):** (a) **RED-equivalent regex probe** across all 7 names (`grepl`, ignore.case, perl — mirrors R CMD build's case-insensitive relative-path match): OLD exact patterns MISS every dupe form (the leak); NEW `.*` form matches `<NAME> 2.md` AND `<NAME> copy.md`, does NOT over-match `<NAME>.Rmd`/`<NAME>_archive.txt`, canonical names stay excluded — all 7 OK. (b) **Build-equivalent (authoritative):** staged **14 real spaced dupes** (both forms × 7 names), ran `R CMD build --no-build-vignettes --no-manual .` (RC=0); the resulting `nprcgenekeepr_1.1.0.9000.tar.gz` (693 files) contained **zero** of the 7 names as `.md` → all dupes + canonicals excluded, real content (DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE) present. `trap cleanup EXIT` removed all 14 dupes + the tarball; `git status` clean except the intended mods + the 2 pre-existing untracked (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`). Did NOT run a full `devtools::check()` — the WARNING is a pure function of tarball contents (verified directly); S58 established the dupe→WARNING link firsthand and self-dinged the redundant check.
  - **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only (Session 59 entry under `[Unreleased]`; dev-process history, not a user-facing release note → NOT `NEWS.md`). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** no dupe present this session; explicit `git add` of only `.Rbuildignore` + `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

**⟢ Carried — Session 58 (PERMANENT `.Rbuildignore` SESSION_NOTES dupe-fix, DONE; full detail below):**
**Task (Session 58 — 2026-06-12):** Apply the `.Rbuildignore` permanent dupe-fix (Session 57 handoff SUGGESTED-NEXT #1) — broaden `^SESSION_NOTES\.md$` → `^SESSION_NOTES.*\.md$` so future macOS sync dupes (`SESSION_NOTES 2.md`, …) never re-enter the build tarball and re-raise the R CMD check "non-portable file names" WARNING. **(DONE.)**
**Started:** 2026-06-12
**Status:** **DONE.** Build-config-only change (one-line `.Rbuildignore` broadening), **TDD phase = N/A** declared every response — no production R code and no shippable unit test (`.Rbuildignore` is dropped from the built tarball, so a `testthat` assertion on it can't run under R CMD check); RED→GREEN→REFACTOR does not apply. One close-out commit on `add-methodology`.
  - **The fix:** `.Rbuildignore:77` `^SESSION_NOTES\.md$` → `^SESSION_NOTES.*\.md$`. This is the PERMANENT version of S57's manual `rm` (the fix S57 explicitly DEFERRED in Learning 57b) — it generalizes the same narrow class the project already patched once for `.Rhistory` (`.Rbuildignore:30` `^\.Rhistory\ 2$`). Resolves S57 gotcha (1) at the root: the dupe is now build-ignored whether or not it exists on disk, so it can never again re-raise the WARNING.
  - **Verification (firsthand, authoritative):** (a) **RED-equivalent regex probe** (`grepl`, ignore.case, perl — mirrors R CMD build's case-insensitive relative-path match): OLD matches `SESSION_NOTES.md` only; `SESSION_NOTES 2.md`/`3.md`/`copy.md` all **FALSE** (the leak path); NEW matches all of them TRUE and over-matches nothing (`CHANGELOG.md`, `SESSION_NOTES_archive.txt` both FALSE — canonical `SESSION_NOTES.md` still excluded, non-`.md` untouched). (b) **Build-equivalent (authoritative for THIS WARNING):** staged a real empty `SESSION_NOTES 2.md`, ran `R CMD build --no-build-vignettes --no-manual .` (RC=0); `tar tzf` of the resulting `nprcgenekeepr_1.1.0.9000.tar.gz` → **zero** SESSION_NOTES entries (693 files total) → the dupe is excluded, so R CMD check's "non-portable file names" check cannot fire on it. Did NOT run a full `devtools::check()`: the WARNING is a pure function of tarball contents (verified directly), no R code/metadata affecting check changed, and S57 already established the dupe→WARNING link firsthand — a full check would repeat S57's self-flagged over-check ding. Temp dupe + tarball removed; `git status` clean except the intended mods + 2 pre-existing untracked (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).
  - **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only (Session 58 entry under `[Unreleased]`; dev-process history, not a user-facing release note → NOT `NEWS.md`). **[macos-dupe-scan]:** no dupe present this session (S57's removal held); explicit `git add` of only `.Rbuildignore` + `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

Edited only `.Rbuildignore` (build config; not shipped/installed) and docs (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES). No `R/` code → no app boot; the authoritative gate is the clean `R CMD build` + tarball inspection above.

**Started:** 2026-06-11 · **Completed:** 2026-06-12
**Status:** **DONE.** Two commits on `add-methodology`: **`17e3fa06`** (`checkRequiredCols` RED→GREEN→REFACTOR) + **`69c8d759`** (5 refactors + de-exclude). The §4 line-specific-exclusion deliverable of #30 is now COMPLETE — every R-file line exclusion is gone except the deliberately-kept `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard`.
  - **`checkRequiredCols.R` (#1, genuine RED→GREEN→REFACTOR):** RED test pins the owner-chosen robust NA contract (`c("dam","sex","birth",NA)`→`c("id","sire")`; `c("id",NA)`→`c("sire","dam","sex","birth")` with reportErrors=TRUE — errored before with `"missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed"`). GREEN = `requiredCols[!requiredCols %in% cols]` + delete the `.lintr` entry. REFACTOR = `@details` documents the intentional robust NA behavior + `document()`. Non-NA byte-identical; reportErrors=FALSE untouched.
  - **3 fully behavior-none REFACTORs:** `correctParentSex.R` (if/else inverted to a guard clause — report body falls through), `fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R` (`else { if }`→`else if`, inline `# nolint` deleted), `setExit.R` (`mapply`→`unlist(Map(...))` — `chooseDate` always length-1). Each fix + de-exclude in the same change ([lint-net-zero]).
  - **`addSexAndAgeToGroup.R` (#11) — DOWNGRADED from the plan's RED slice to a REFACTOR, then ADOPTED-ROBUST:** my firsthand probe proved `sapply`→`ped$sex[match(ids,ped$id)]` behavior-none on the documented domain (the `age` vapply already errors identically on any ≠1-row id, so the planned `match()`-error concern doesn't occur). BUT the adversarial pass found it diverges on **empty `ids`**: old `sapply` dropped the `sex` column (2-col result) → CRASHED the sole caller `modBreedingGroups.R:438` (`colnames(gp) <- c(<3 names>)`); new keeps the documented 3-col schema → empty table renders. **Owner ADOPTED the new behavior** (latent crash-fix + matches `@return`); pinned by an empty-ids contract test + a happy-path characterization test; `@details` documents it.
  - **`create_wkbk.R` (#13) — ACCEPTED cosmetic divergence:** inner `if(replace)`→`if(!replace)` guard clause. On a non-logical non-coercible `replace` while the file exists, both error but the message text differs (`"argument is not interpretable as logical"`→`"invalid argument type"`); `replace` is documented logical, coercible values identical. **Owner ACCEPTED** (out-of-contract; no clean lint-passing rewrite preserves the message).
  - **Verification (all firsthand/authoritative):** `lint_package()` = **0**; the 6 files lint-clean (`parse_settings=FALSE`); full suite **0 fail / 0 err / 159 skip** (S49 baseline + 5 new passing expectations → zero regression); `devtools::check()` **0 errors** (1 WARNING + 2 NOTEs — all pre-existing stray-file/clock-skew, chiefly the macOS `SESSION_NOTES 2.md` dupe; NOT mine). **Adversarial verification** `wf_168f8dcf-1e5` (6 skeptics, each told to REFUTE + run R): 4 fully equivalent (6000-iter fuzz / 146 seeded / 21 / ~50 inputs), the 2 divergences owner-reviewed. **Phase-3E:** `addSexAndAgeToGroup`'s runtime path is covered by `test_modBreedingGroups.R:1015-1122` (the `viewGrp` member view → `c("Ego ID","Sex","Age in Years")` colnames), green in the full suite — no app boot needed (pure fn). **Process slip (self-caught):** committed `17e3fa06` after adding `@details` without re-linting → an 81-char line landed in it, fixed-forward in `69c8d759`. **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only. **[macos-dupe-scan]:** explicit `git add` (excluded `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`, `SESSION_NOTES 2.md`). #30 stays OPEN (close decision below).

The 6: `convertFromCenter.R`/`fillGroupMembers.R`/`hasGenotype.R` (`unnecessary_nesting` collapses — drop an `else` after an unconditional `stop()`/`return()`; `else { if }`→`else if`); `getLkDirectAncestors.R`/`getLkDirectRelatives.R` (rename local var `source`→`msgSource` — `undesirable_function_linter` flags the *symbol* shadowing `base::source`; also deleted the 2 now-redundant inline `# nolint`s in the latter); `saveDataframesAsFiles.R` (`unnecessary_lambda` → `vapply(dfList, inherits, logical(1L), what = "data.frame")`). **A per-file adversarial-verifier workflow (`wf_eb654565-758`, 7 skeptics each told to REFUTE behavior-equivalence + allowed to run R) returned refuted=FALSE for all 6** (each with empirical before/after R comparison). **The 7th planned file, `checkRequiredCols.R` (§4A #1), was RECLASSIFIED to Phase 4 mid-session:** the verifier + my firsthand repro proved its `sapply`→`%in%` "behavior-none" fix actually changes EXPORTED error semantics — on out-of-contract `NA`-in-`cols` the old `if (!any(col == cols))` throws `"missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed"` while `%in%` returns the missing cols cleanly (non-NA output byte-identical, 5 cases). Per the strict-TDD REFACTOR contract (no behavior change) I paused, surfaced 3 options via `AskUserQuestion`; **owner chose DEFER to Phase 4** (RED→GREEN→REFACTOR w/ an NA-contract test) → I reverted that file to its exact pre-session state (sapply + exclusion restored). **Owner ALSO flagged the `.lintr` casing bug** (`R/CheckRequiredCols.R` capital-C suppresses nothing on case-sensitive CI) → fixed to lowercase `R/checkRequiredCols.R` (config-only, behavior-none; the deferred file's L34 lint now suppressed on Linux too). **Verification (all firsthand):** `lint_package()` = 0; the 6 files lint-clean under `parse_settings=FALSE`; full suite **2140 pass / 0 fail / 0 err / 159 skip** (= S49 baseline EXACTLY → zero regression); `devtools::check()` **0 errors** (1 WARNING + 1 NOTE both from stray top-level files — chiefly the macOS dupe **`SESSION_NOTES 2.md`**, 563 KB, untracked, created 16:49; NOT from this change); `man/` untouched (no roxygen edits). **Plan updated** to reflect the reclassification (§4A #1, §5 Phase 3 done / Phase 4 +`checkRequiredCols`, §3 casing resolved, §6 dragon #6). **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only. **[macos-dupe-scan]:** explicit `git add` (excluded `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`, `SESSION_NOTES 2.md`). #30 stays OPEN.

**⟢ Carried — Session 52 (fix #42 pkgdown, DONE; full detail below):**
**Task (Session 52 — 2026-06-11):** FIX issue #42 — the `pkgdown` workflow's Build-site step fails on `master` because `docs/methodology/` + `docs/planning/` are git-tracked INSIDE pkgdown's default `docs/` output dir, so a fresh CI clone's `docs/` holds those files + NO `pkgdown.yml` sentinel → `clean_site()`/`check_dest_is_pkgdown()` refuses to wipe a dir it didn't build → exit 1. Deliverable = make `pkgdown` go GREEN on master. (DONE)
**Started:** 2026-06-11
**Status:** **DONE — pkgdown is GREEN on master (run `27362288625`, commit `c6ad23dd`: Build site SUCCESS + Deploy SUCCESS); #42 CLOSED.** Chose **Option 2 (repoint pkgdown), NOT the issue's recommended Option 1 (relocate the doc trees)** — surfaced the override via `AskUserQuestion` because Option 1 conflicts with the methodology framework's own `docs/methodology/` convention: the SYNCED `methodology_dashboard.py:109-110` scores `docs/methodology`+`docs/methodology/workstreams` (20 of the 98 health pts) and the SYNCED `SESSION_RUNNER.md` (13 refs) + `SAFEGUARDS.md` (1 ref) cross-link that path — none durably editable in-repo (re-synced from upstream). **Load-bearing pkgdown internal (verified from installed pkgdown 2.1.1 source + empirically):** `build_site_github_pages()` does `as_pkgdown(pkg, override = list(destination = dest_dir))`, so its `dest_dir="docs"` ARG **overrides** any `_pkgdown.yml destination:` → setting the yml ALONE does NOT fix CI (owner proposed the yml; I confirmed it's necessary-but-insufficient and set BOTH). **Fix (commit `fcc154e8`):** `.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml` — pass `dest_dir="pkgdown_site"` to `build_site_github_pages` + deploy `folder: docs → pkgdown_site`; `_pkgdown.yml` — `destination: pkgdown_site` (keeps local `build_site()` consistent); `.gitignore += pkgdown_site/`; `.Rbuildignore += ^pkgdown_site$`. NO file moves; `docs/methodology`/`docs/planning`/dashboard/synced cross-refs untouched; gh-pages URL unchanged. NO R/ or tests/ change → suite byte-identical, R-CMD-check unaffected (stayed green on all 5 platforms on the PR). **Local proof BEFORE pushing:** simulated the fresh-clone state in a temp package skeleton → `clean_site(dest=docs)` ERRORS "is non-empty and not built by pkgdown" (reproduces #42); `clean_site(dest=pkgdown_site)` = clean no-op. **The fix UNMASKED a separate pre-existing latent bug** (FM-#24-cousin: clean_site was the FIRST of two failures): with clean_site resolved the build reached vignette rendering and failed on `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd:209-229` chunk `make-errorList-definition-tbl` — `data.frame(Error=names(getEmptyErrorLst()), Definition=errorDescriptions)` paired **10 error types vs 9 hardcoded descriptions** ("arguments imply differing number of rows: 10, 9"). Root cause: the **NEW-45 "no period in IDs" feature** (commit `5e228bd9`) added the `invalidIdChars` type to `getEmptyErrorLst()` without updating this vignette table. **Owner chose (via `AskUserQuestion`) to fix the vignette too** (vs file-separately) → added the missing `invalidIdChars` description in positional order (after `duplicateIds`, before `changedCols`) using the package's canonical wording, commit `e89975c8`. **Why latent / why R-CMD-check was green but pkgdown red:** `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` is `.Rbuildignore`'d (`:36`) — R CMD build/check NEVER builds it (likely excluded for CRAN tarball-size/time: it reads screenshots from the also-`.Rbuildignore`'d `vignettes/shiny_app_use/`), but **pkgdown builds vignettes from the source tree, ignoring `.Rbuildignore`**, so it was the ONE pkgdown-built vignette R-CMD-check doesn't cover → the only place a render bug could hide (the other 3 — a2interactive, a3manual, simulatedKValues — ARE check-built → green → render fine). **Validation chain (all firsthand):** PR #43 pkgdown run `27361729368` (fresh-clone, pull_request, Deploy skipped) = SUCCESS, all 4 vignettes rendered; merged PR #43 `--merge` → master `c6ad23dd`; master push run `27362288625` = Build site SUCCESS **+ Deploy SUCCESS** (exercises the new `folder: pkgdown_site` → gh-pages). **#42 auto-closed on merge** (PR body "Fixes #42") + added a validation comment (`#42 issuecomment-4682879576`). **Phase-3E = the live master pkgdown run itself** (real fresh runner, real build+deploy). **Commits on `add-methodology`:** `fcc154e8` (config fix) + `e89975c8` (vignette fix) + this close-out (SESSION_NOTES + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS #52), explicit `git add` ([macos-dupe-scan]: `.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` excluded). **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only.

**Session 51 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 52): Score 9/10.** What helped: S51's handoff named #42 as the suggested next with the EXACT root cause (`docs/methodology`+`docs/planning` tracked in pkgdown's `docs/` → no sentinel → clean_site refuses), the 3 fix options + Option-1 recommendation, the precise key files (`.gitignore:32-36`, `_pkgdown.yml` "no destination → defaults to docs/", `.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml`), and the gotchas (a) R-CMD-check GREEN — don't mistake the pkgdown red for a package-correctness problem, (b) lint #30 + codecov independent of #42. All of (a)/(b) held and saved triage time. What was missing (the ding, and the reason I diverged from the recommendation): S51 recommended **Option 1** without weighing that the methodology framework PINS reference docs to `docs/methodology/` (synced dashboard scoring + synced SESSION_RUNNER/SAFEGUARDS cross-refs) — so Option 1 would break things I can't durably fix; and it didn't anticipate the LATENT vignette bug behind the clean_site failure (it couldn't — that was invisible until clean_site was resolved). What was wrong: nothing factual; the Option-1 rec was sound on its face but superseded by the synced-convention constraint, which I surfaced. ROI: very high.

**Self-assessment (Session 52): 9/10.** Research before action: oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard, git, issues), then read the issue, the sync mechanism (no `bin/sync` in-repo → external tooling I can't inspect → makes the relocate-vs-repoint choice genuinely owner-gated), the full 17-file reference inventory classified synced-vs-owned, and the pkgdown internals (read `build_site_github_pages` source AND empirically proved the `dest_dir` override) BEFORE proposing a fix. Verified, didn't assume: proved the fix with a faithful fresh-clone SIMULATION locally (reproduced the exact #42 error, showed the fix is a no-op) THEN with a real fresh-clone CI run, then with the master push (Build+Deploy). Did NOT silently follow OR silently override the owner's written Option-1 recommendation — surfaced the synced-convention conflict + the pkgdown override via `AskUserQuestion` with exact actions spelled out. Honored the owner's mid-stream correction (the yml `destination`) precisely — confirmed it necessary-but-insufficient and set both yml + workflow arg. When the fix unmasked a SECOND bug, did NOT scope-creep silently — surfaced it (root-caused to NEW-45, characterized the whack-a-mole risk + the .Rbuildignore reason it was latent) and let the owner choose to fix-or-defer. Stakeholder decisions posed: 4 (fix-approach, merge, vignette-scope, plus honoring the yml correction) — all answered as posed; 0 corrections. Minor ding: a truly exhaustive pre-push audit might have rendered all 4 vignettes locally to catch the ColonyManagerTutorial bug before the first CI cycle (I judged local render unreliable vs CI's fresh `local::.` install — defensible, but it cost one CI round-trip). Met/exceeded the S47–S51 bar.

**⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = #30 (lintr cleanup) — the last standing CI red.** With #42 closed, the pkgdown workflow is GREEN on master; the ONLY remaining red across the CI matrix is **lint** (issue #30, known/accepted style debt — it failed on PR #43 as expected). Greening lint would make the entire master CI matrix green. It's an enhancement-labeled cleanup (`lintr` until satisfied), naturally incremental. Other standing backlog candidates unchanged: **#34** (integrate `qcStudbook()` in modInput, bug), **#38/#32/#26** (configurable auto-gen ID format), **#37** (exported-but-unused functions). **Branch model (unchanged from S51):** the #42 fix is now ON master via PR #43 (`c6ad23dd`); **`add-methodology` remains the historical dev branch** and holds THIS close-out (master got the fix commits via the merge but NOT these close-out notes — they live on add-methodology; the next add-methodology→master sync or PR carries them). Repo left checked out on `add-methodology`. **Key files (the #42 fix, for reference):** `.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml` (`dest_dir`/`folder` at the Build site + Deploy steps), `_pkgdown.yml` (`destination: pkgdown_site`), `.gitignore` (`pkgdown_site/`), `.Rbuildignore` (`^pkgdown_site$`), `vignettes/ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd:209-230` (the error-table chunk). **Gotchas for the next session:** (a) pkgdown builds ALL vignettes from source IGNORING `.Rbuildignore` — so a `.Rbuildignore`'d vignette can break pkgdown while R-CMD-check stays green; if you add/edit error types in `getEmptyErrorLst()`, update the `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` table in lockstep (positional pairing). (b) `build_site_github_pages(dest_dir=...)` OVERRIDES `_pkgdown.yml destination:` — change the workflow ARG, not just the yml, to move pkgdown's CI output dir. (c) the local `docs/` still holds the old Dec-2025 pkgdown build + the tracked methodology/planning; pkgdown no longer writes there — harmless cruft. (d) `methodology_dashboard.py`, `SESSION_RUNNER.md`, `SAFEGUARDS.md` are SYNCED (don't durably edit; customize via CLAUDE.md).

**⟢ Carried — Session 50 (8e-7 CI per-module fresh-process grouping, DONE; full detail below):**
**Task (Session 50 — 2026-06-10):** IMPLEMENT slice **8e-7** (issue #40, sub-plan §8e-7) — make `.github/workflows/shinytest2.yaml` run the 23 `^(app|e2e)-` E2E test files in **per-module groups, each in a fresh R process**, to defang the 23-in-one-process Chrome flake (the S34 `[flake-aware-validation]` finding). TDD shape: **run-and-observe** (CI config; no RED→GREEN), gated `PRE-RED→run-and-observe` via `AskUserQuestion`. The group partition is statically checkable locally (select EXACTLY the 23 files, no overlap/gap, replicating testthat's stripped-name match — Learning #33c); the flake fix itself is **live-GitHub-runner-only** → ships UNVALIDATED locally (state in handoff, FM #24's cousin). (DONE)
**Started:** 2026-06-10
**Status:** **DONE.** Converted the single 23-files-in-one-process E2E run step in `.github/workflows/shinytest2.yaml` into a **single job that loops over 13 per-module group regexes, each in a FRESH `Rscript` process** (owner-gated topology — single-job loop **over** a 13-leg matrix via `AskUserQuestion`; plan §8 "loop spawning Rscript per group" — 1× setup, faithful + cheapest). Caps any one R process at **≤3 files** (vs 23) → defuses the S34 "AppDriver process-count dragon" (~1 transient error / 5 full-tier single-process runs). **Per group:** `test_dir(filter=rx, stop_on_failure=FALSE)` → `cat` a per-group `passed/failed/skipped/error` report → **fail/error>0 ⇒ `quit(status=1)` (checked FIRST)** → **passed==0 ⇒ `stop()` silent-skip guard** (now PER GROUP — stronger than the old whole-run guard; a single-group filter drift can't hide behind the others; a ZERO-match regex is separately caught by `test_dir`'s own "No test files found" abort). The bash loop runs ALL groups (full signal, one flake doesn't skip the rest) and reds the job if ANY group failed — **preserving the `stop_on_failure` job semantics** + the job env / Chrome provisioning / `R CMD INSTALL` / `timeout-minutes:30` / removed `continue-on-error` (R6); each group wrapped in `::group::`/`::endgroup::` log folds. **TDD = run-and-observe** (CI config; no RED→GREEN, plan §6), gated `PRE-RED→run-and-observe` via `AskUserQuestion`. **[ci-static-proof] partition verified locally** (Learning #33c — replicate testthat's stripped-name `grepl` match): extracted the 13 regexes from the COMMITTED YAML and proved they select EXACTLY the 23 `^(app|e2e)-` files — **union==tier, no overlap, no gap, no stray** — applied against the full 182-file dir (the `app` near-misses `test_appServer_dynamicTabs`/`test_create_test_app` correctly untouched by `^app-`'s trailing dash). **YAML parses** (`yaml.safe_load`); **run-step `bash -n` clean**; **[quote-safety] the `Rscript -e '...'` block has ZERO single-quotes** (all R strings double-quoted; comments apostrophe-free — an apostrophe would close the bash single-quote and break the step; grep-confirmed). **[phase-3E-smoke] run-step LOGIC smoked firsthand** on a throwaway test dir (deterministic, no Chrome): pass→exit0, fail→exit1 (NOT mislabeled "executed nothing" — the fail-first reorder), skip→silent-skip guard→exit1, nomatch→`test_dir` abort→exit1 (all 4 branches). **⚠ The FLAKE mitigation itself is LIVE-RUNNER-ONLY** (environmental — FM #24's cousin): partition/guard/exit logic proven locally, but only the first live GitHub run can confirm the 23-in-one-process flake is gone — **NOT claimed fixed until a live run shows it.** **Scope = CI-config ONLY** (`shinytest2.yaml` + these notes); NO `R/`/`tests/` change → the suite is byte-identical, no regression run needed (the change alters only how CI INVOKES the suite). **[news-vs-changelog]** → CHANGELOG only. **Commit:** one `ci:`/`docs:` close-out (`shinytest2.yaml` + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS #50 + these notes) via explicit `git add` ([macos-dupe-scan]: `.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` EXCLUDED). **Did NOT push or bundle the master push (separate deliverable; FM #2/#18).** Re-proof scripts (NOT committed, transient): `/tmp/verify_8e7_yaml.R` (committed-list partition), `/tmp/smoke_8e7.sh` (run-step logic).

The data-bearing design **PAIRS** the button LABEL (matched via active-pane innerText — absent until the nested tab is activated) with the `suspendWhenHidden` rendered DT (needs BOTH group formation AND tab visibility) ⇒ each block genuinely needs the full flow, not just nested-tab nav. The spike proved the intermediate state (post-form, pre-activation → `Ego ID`/`Export Current Group` still FALSE). **`animalSource="all"`** uses `ped$id` directly, ISOLATING breeding from the GVA dependency (the `topRanked` branch's `req(geneticValues())`, `appServer.R:272`; `shared$geneticValues` is only set after a GVA run). **Assertions (Option-C STRUCTURAL, seed-INDEPENDENT — GREEN with NO `NPRC_BG_SEED`):** D5 `assert_active_pane(.,"Export Current Group")` + `grepl("Ego ID", get_html_safe("#breedingGroups-groupMemberTable"))`; T7 `"Export Current Group"` + `"Age in Years"`; T9 `"Export Current Group Kinship Matrix"` + `grepl("<table", get_html_safe("#breedingGroups-groupKinTable"))`. **NOT asserted:** group COUNT (requested `numGp=3` but the algo formed ONE big MIS group — the S43-handoff's `{numGp,numGp+1}` is not guaranteed) and the within-group kinship invariant (unattainable: module hardcodes `ignore=F–F`). **GREEN flow** (per block): `upload_and_wait(app, fixture)` → `navigate_to_tab("Breeding Groups")` → `set_inputs(animalSource="all", nIterations=5, wait_=FALSE)` → `click_element_safe("#breedingGroups-formGroups")` → `wait_for_module_ready("breedingGroups", timeout=180000)` → `click_element_safe("a[data-value='Group Detail']")`. **Verification:** D5/T7/T9 all GREEN live (detailed **8/0/0**, tutorial **11/0/0**); **[mutation-check] 13/13 ALL PASS** (`/tmp/mutation_8e6c.R`: correct→TRUE; wrong export label + imaginary column + **right-token-WRONG-table** (`"Ego ID"` in the kin DT) → FALSE; foreign-pane (Pedigree Browser) → FALSE; pre-flow RED re-confirm → FALSE). **Non-e2e regression 2140 `expectation_success` / 0 failed / 0 error / 159 skip / 5 `modPyramid` warn / 0 non-e2e offenders — proven byte-IDENTICAL pre- and post-edit via a `git stash` diff** (the edit touches only e2e blocks, which skip at `create_test_app()` before any assertion ⇒ structurally inert on the non-e2e count). **⚠ The 2140-vs-S48's-2180 is a MEASUREMENT-METHOD difference, NOT a regression:** `pkgload::load_all` under renv → 2140/0-err (clean) vs `library()` under `RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=false` (bare system lib) → ERRORs on tests whose Suggests deps the system lib lacks; S48's 2180 was the other method. **Phase-3E:** the live GREEN AppDriver runs (real upload→QC→kinship→group-formation→Group-Detail render across the 2 files) + the 13/13 mutation spike ARE the runtime (#31). **Session 48 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 49):** **Score 9/10.** What helped: the handoff named the exact slice (8e-6c), the exact blocks+lines (D5 `:89`, T7 `:135`, T9 `:178`), the spike recipe (boot→upload→nav→formGroups→ready→activate Group Detail), the **explicit "recon left the nested-tab id + `viewGrp` selector OPEN — capture FIRSTHAND"** flag (which correctly told me NOT to trust a remembered id — and indeed the tabset has NO id, so the real answer was the DOM-link selector I found in the spike), the `ignore=F–F`-unattainable + "assert export-button presence" steer (kept me from asserting the kinship invariant), the `{numGp,numGp+1}` caveat (explicitly hedged "NOT a hard +1" — which saved me, since the algo actually formed ONE group), the `suspendWhenHidden` note, the browser env vars, and the SYSTEM-lib "verify firsthand, reinstall ONLY if R/ changed" method. All load-bearing facts held under firsthand spiking. What was missing (minor): the handoff phrased it as "capture its `tabsetPanel` id" (assuming an id exists) — there is none, so the activation is a DOM click; and it didn't anticipate the regression measurement-method sensitivity (2140 vs 2180) — I resolved that with a stash diff. What was wrong: `{numGp,numGp+1}` (actual = 1 group) — but it was appropriately hedged and steered away from asserting count, so it never misled. ROI: very high — "spike firsthand BEFORE RED" + the OPEN-items flag + the "assert export-button presence" steer produced a clean session with 0 stakeholder corrections.

Minor: I first mis-read system-lib currency via plain `library()` (renv-redirected → `gatedSeed FALSE`) before re-checking under the renv-bypass — a momentary detour, self-corrected in the same step. Met/exceeded the S47/S48 bar.

The `gvResults` eventReactive's `setDataReady` (on `#geneticValue-moduleContainer`) fires because the default-visible Rankings inner tab reads `gvaView()→gvResults()` on the click → `wait_for_module_ready` is the right barrier (NO inner-tabset switch needed; Summary/Visualizations stay `suspendWhenHidden`).
